target.title: [AI-assisted] Add session history family lookup
target.body:
## Problem

When a threaded conversation has already been split across reset transcripts, `chat.history` and the `sessions_history` tool only read the currently active transcript. The session store can already retain the family through `usageFamilySessionIds`, but history readers do not use that chain and do not discover archived `*.jsonl.reset.<timestamp>` topic transcripts.

That means an agent/tool can be looking at the correct logical Telegram/Slack/Discord thread and still miss earlier turns after session rollover.

## Fix

Add an opt-in family history path:

- `chat.history` accepts `includeFamily: true`.
- `sessions_history` accepts `includeFamily`.
- Gateway and embedded `chat.history` now share the bounded/header-checked reset archive resolver instead of using separate prefix scans.
- The shared resolver can include owned topic-suffixed reset archives such as `<sessionId>-topic-456.jsonl.reset.<timestamp>` only when the archive header matches the requested session id.
- Same-prefix colliding topic archives and headerless topic archives are ignored.
- Family read targets are capped before transcript reads, and normal `chat.history` behavior remains opt-in: UI startup does not load ancestor history.

## Validation

Current head: `1aa4e9cbb94f6841803bd55559aa0ea6de17dc67`.

- `CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts src/agents/tools/embedded-gateway-stub.test.ts`
  - gateway shard: 84 tests passed
  - agents shard: 11 tests passed
- `CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts`
  - 36 tests passed
- `CI=true node --import tsx scripts/generate-prompt-snapshots.ts --check`
  - `Prompt snapshots are current (7 files).`
- `CI=true node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo`
  - passed
- `CI=true node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo`
  - passed
- `git diff --check`
  - passed, no output

## Real Behavior Proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** A logical thread whose active session has `usageFamilySessionIds` should be able to fetch earlier reset transcript turns when a caller explicitly requests family history, including owned topic-suffixed reset archives, without reading colliding or headerless topic archives. The current active transcript must also be preserved when a retained reset family has more entries than the bounded 32 read targets.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout on this PR branch at current head `a12e1bbcf0`, using temporary on-disk session directories, the real Gateway `chat.history` RPC integration harness, embedded-mode `chat.history`, and the same reset archive filename/header patterns that OpenClaw creates for topic transcripts.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  1. Created temporary session directories and session-store entries with `usageFamilySessionIds` linking an ancestor session and the current session.
  2. Wrote an owned plain ancestor reset archive and an owned topic-suffixed ancestor archive with matching `type: "session"` headers.
  3. Wrote newer same-prefix colliding and headerless topic reset archives in the same sessions directory.
  4. Invoked the real Gateway `chat.history` RPC path with and without `includeFamily: true`.
  5. Created a second Gateway/RPC case with 40 ancestor session ids before the current id in `usageFamilySessionIds`.
  6. Invoked embedded-mode `chat.history(includeFamily)` through the sessions tool stub, including the same >32-family-target cap case.
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal capture from current head:
  ```text
  $ CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts src/agents/tools/embedded-gateway-stub.test.ts
  Test Files  2 passed (2)
  Tests  84 passed (84)
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
  Tests  11 passed (11)
  [test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 44.69s

  $ CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
  Tests  36 passed (36)
  [test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 30.16s

  $ CI=true node --import tsx scripts/generate-prompt-snapshots.ts --check
  Prompt snapshots are current (7 files).

  $ CI=true node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
  # passed

  $ CI=true node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo
  # passed

  $ git diff --check
  # no output
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** Default `chat.history` excludes ancestor reset archive content, while `chat.history(includeFamily)` includes the owned plain ancestor archive, includes the owned topic-suffixed ancestor archive, includes current-session content, and rejects the colliding/headerless topic reset archives. In the >32-target Gateway/RPC regression, `chat.history(includeFamily)` contains `current survives family target cap`, contains early ancestor content, and does not read the ancestor entries beyond the target cap. Embedded `chat.history(includeFamily)` now reads the current active transcript first, then current archives, then ancestor archives/active transcripts; its >32-target regression also keeps the current transcript and drops over-cap ancestors.
- **What was not tested:** I did not run a live Telegram topic end-to-end, and this PR does not add prompt-level carryover summaries or automatic prompt injection. It only adds explicit family history retrieval for gateway/tool callers.

## Current head addendum: `8c164eeaa3`

ClawSweeper's `a12e1bbcf0` review finding is addressed in this head:

- Family target selection still reserves the current active transcript before the 32-target cap is reached.
- Family output order is now ancestor transcripts first, then current-session reset archives, then the current active transcript, so final `limit` trimming keeps the active session tail.
- Embedded `chat.history(includeFamily)` mirrors the Gateway order.
- Added over-limit regressions proving `includeFamily` with a small final limit keeps current active history.

Standalone redacted embedded `chat.history` smoke from current head `8c164eeaa3545a9f05dc507908f9dec0236d2c47`:

```text
$ node --import tsx --input-type=module <<'TS'
# Creates a temporary session.store, one ancestor transcript, and one current transcript;
# calls createEmbeddedCallGateway().chat.history with default, includeFamily, and includeFamily+limit.
TS
{
  "default": [
    "redacted current context"
  ],
  "includeFamily": [
    "redacted ancestor context",
    "redacted current context"
  ],
  "includeFamilyLimit1": [
    "redacted current context"
  ]
}
```

Validation on `8c164eeaa3545a9f05dc507908f9dec0236d2c47`:

```text
$ CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts src/agents/tools/embedded-gateway-stub.test.ts
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  84 passed (84)
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  11 passed (11)

$ CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts -t 'chat.history preserves current transcript when family targets hit the cap'
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  1 passed | 41 skipped (42)

$ CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tools/embedded-gateway-stub.test.ts -t 'keeps embedded current chat history when family targets hit the cap'
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  2 passed | 20 skipped (22)

$ CI=true node --import tsx scripts/generate-prompt-snapshots.ts --check
Prompt snapshots are current (7 files).

$ CI=true node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
# passed

$ CI=true node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo
# passed

$ git diff --check
# no output
```

## Current head addendum: `0d37b42702`

Maintainer readiness note addressed on current head `0d37b42702688ac9c4bf0edf949f99b00f096cf6`:

- Removed the `resetAncestors` sessions_history alias so the model-facing selector is only `includeFamily`.
- Kept Gateway `chat.history` and `sessions_history` aligned on the single `includeFamily` flag.
- The embedded history macOS path-normalization failure was already addressed by dropping the redundant path assertion in `6050d0bbfb`.
- Exact-head CI has restarted for `0d37b42702`; at refresh time there were no failed checks and the remaining required/security checks were still running.

Validation on `0d37b42702688ac9c4bf0edf949f99b00f096cf6`:

```text
$ CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  36 passed (36)

$ CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tools/embedded-gateway-stub.test.ts -t 'reads embedded chat history from reset ancestor transcripts when includeFamily is set|keeps embedded current chat history when family targets hit the cap'
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  4 passed | 18 skipped (22)

$ CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts -t 'chat.history includes owned topic reset archives when includeFamily is set|chat.history preserves current transcript when family targets hit the cap'
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  1 passed | 41 skipped (42)

$ git diff --check
# no output
```

## Current head addendum: `1aa4e9cbb9`

ClawSweeper's generated-snapshot blocker is addressed on current head `1aa4e9cbb94f6841803bd55559aa0ea6de17dc67`:

- Refreshed the Codex dynamic-tool prompt snapshots after removing `resetAncestors`.
- `resetAncestors` no longer appears in source, docs, protocol/tool snapshots, or prompt snapshot counters.
- Kept `includeFamily` as the only model-facing family-history selector.

Validation on `1aa4e9cbb94f6841803bd55559aa0ea6de17dc67`:

target.title: fix(agents): sync stale this.model snapshot after /model switch
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #92415 — AgentSession.this.model goes stale when /model switch writes to session store but the in-memory session never re-reads it.

## Root Cause

applyModelOverrideToSessionEntry() in model-overrides.ts writes a providerOverride/modelOverride + liveModelSwitchPending to the session store. The deferred switch mechanism (shouldSwitchToLiveModel()) re-reads the store and throws LiveSessionModelSwitchError on the next prompt, which triggers a run restart with a fresh AgentSession. That restart path was already safe.

**But** between the /model command and the restart, the long-lived AgentSession (TUI interactive, plugin SDK) uses the stale this.model for identity operations — provider lookup, model config, registry keys — producing wrong behavior.

The embedded runner was **not affected**: it creates a fresh AgentSession per run with params.model derived from the session entry at Attempt.ts:2358.

## Fix

Add syncModelFromStoreEntry() called at the start of prompt() before model validation. Guard chain:

1. Skip if storePath is undefined (services path, no session store)
2. Skip if liveModelSwitchPending is not true
3. Skip if providerOverride or modelOverride is missing in store
4. Skip if model already matches (prevents duplicate appendModelChange)
5. Look up model in registry — skip if not found
6. Check auth is configured for target provider/model — skip if not
7. Direct assignment to this.model (not setModel() — avoids duplicate transcript entries and settings writes)
8. Call appendModelChange() and setThinkingLevel()

## Files Changed

- \src/agents/sessions/agent-session.ts\ — Add storePath/sessionKey constructor params, syncModelFromStoreEntry() method, call site in prompt()
- \src/agents/sessions/sdk.ts\ — Plumb storePath/sessionKey through CreateAgentSessionOptions
- \src/agents/sessions/agent-session-services.ts\ — Same pass-through for services path
- \src/agents/sessions/agent-session.model-sync.test.ts\ — 10-test suite expanded to 20 tests (agents-core + agents-support)

## Verification

- [x] 20 new tests pass (agents-core + agents-support pools)
- [x] 23 existing live-model-switch.test.ts tests pass
- [x] 204 sessions-patch.test.ts tests pass
- [x] TS clean
- [x] All callers of applyModelOverrideToSessionEntry() verified safe

**Supersedes:** #92388 (sheyanmin)

PR #92388 independently identified the same stale-model symptom in checkCompaction() and proposed refreshCurrentModelFromRegistry() as a fix. After analysis, that approach cannot resolve cross-provider model switches because it queries the registry using the stale this.model.provider/id — only finding the old model.

This PR (#93056) fixes the root cause at the prompt() entry point by reading providerOverride/modelOverride from the session store. All downstream consumers (compaction, auth, thinking levels, context metrics) automatically benefit. See #92388 comment for detailed technical comparison.

Closes #92415

---

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** After a cross-provider model switch (e.g., /model openai/gpt-4o → /model anthropic/claude-sonnet-4), the next user message used the stale this.model snapshot instead of the switched model's config. The syncModelFromStoreEntry() fix reads providerOverride/modelOverride from the session store entry at the start of prompt(), before the model-validation block, and applies it unconditionally (not gated on liveModelSwitchPending).

**Real environment tested:**
- **OS:** Windows 11 24H2 (x64)
- **Runtime:** Node.js v24.14.1
- **Branch:** fix/stale-model-after-switch-92415 at 15a82ded66

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
\\\ ash
pnpm vitest run --reporter=verbose src/agents/sessions/agent-session.model-sync.test.ts
\\\

**Evidence after fix:** Terminal output captured after running the patched code:
\\\
✓ agents-core > syncModelFromStoreEntry > applies model override when liveModelSwitchPending is true and model is in registry with auth (623ms)
✓ agents-core > syncModelFromStoreEntry > syncs entry overrides regardless of liveModelSwitchPending flag (263ms)
✓ agents-core > syncModelFromStoreEntry > does not sync when store entry has no overrides (257ms)
✓ agents-core > syncModelFromStoreEntry > does not crash when model not in registry (254ms)
✓ agents-core > syncModelFromStoreEntry > does not sync when providerOverride is missing (252ms)
✓ agents-core > syncModelFromStoreEntry > does not sync when auth not configured for the target model (247ms)
✓ agents-core > syncModelFromStoreEntry > does not crash when storePath is undefined (no-op) (261ms)
✓ agents-core > syncModelFromStoreEntry > skips sync when model already matches the store entry (271ms)
✓ agents-core > syncModelFromStoreEntry > does not sync when modelOverride is missing (providerOverride present) (250ms)
✓ agents-core > syncModelFromStoreEntry > does not crash on readSessionEntry error (245ms)
✓ agents-support > syncModelFromStoreEntry > syncs entry overrides regardless of liveModelSwitchPending flag (291ms)
✓ agents-support > syncModelFromStoreEntry > does not crash when model not in registry (268ms)
✓ agents-support > syncModelFromStoreEntry > does not crash on readSessionEntry error (259ms)

 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  20 passed (20)
   Duration  12.97s
\\\

**Observed result after fix:** All tests pass — model sync correctly applies overrides:
- ✅ Cross-provider switch (test-provider → switched-provider)
- ✅ Unconditional sync regardless of liveModelSwitchPending flag
- ✅ No-op when no overrides exist
- ✅ Graceful handling: model not in registry, missing fields, read errors
- ✅ Idempotency: skip sync when model already matches

**What was not tested:**
- Real (non-mocked) OpenClaw daemon with live API provider — requires a running daemon with configured providers; the pure-mock vitest test covers the code-path correctness without external dependencies
- Cross-process session store updates (where another process writes to sessions.json between prompt() calls)

target.title: fix(channels): expose runtime controls in CLI
target.body:
Fixes #75153

Adds `openclaw channels start`, `openclaw channels stop`, and `openclaw channels restart` so linked channel listeners can be controlled through the existing gateway `channels.start` / `channels.stop` RPCs without rerunning login or clearing auth. `restart` stops the resolved account before starting it again, and runtime control does not trigger channel auth login/logout flows.

Validation:

target.title: fix(agents): prevent recursive task execution during model fallback
target.body:
## Problem

When a session's primary model becomes unavailable mid-session and a fallback model serves a turn that was triggered by an internal completion announcement, the fallback can misread the announced report as a directive and spawn duplicate work. In the originating incident (#92271) a fallback turn converted "background task completed" reports into new `sessions_spawn` tasks at depth 1 — re-finalizing an already-finished PR and pushing junk commits onto its branch. `maxSpawnDepth` does not catch this: the recursion is semantic (re-executing reported results), not structural.

## Invariant

> A fallback turn replying to completion announcements must not re-execute or re-spawn work already reported as completed.

Defined once at the shared fallback/run boundary, then enforced per surface — per the review feedback.

## Changes

1. **Completion announcements are marked as evidence (all harnesses).** `buildAnnounceReplyInstruction` (`src/agents/subagent-announce.ts`) now states in every variant that the announcement reports already-completed work and must not be re-executed or re-delegated, and the previous open-ended "continue the task" wording is narrowed to the original task's own remaining steps. This travels in message content, so embedded, CLI, and MCP-exposed harnesses all receive it.
2. **Fallback turns are identified mechanically.** `runWithModelFallback` / `runWithImageModelFallback` set `isFallback` on non-primary candidates (`ModelFallbackRunOptions`); the bit flows through the embedded run params into tool construction. Primary attempts keep their exact previous call shape.
3. **Work-spawning is blocked at the tool choke point.** `sessions_spawn` returns `forbidden` (with an instructive error) when the current run is a fallback candidate AND the turn was triggered by an internal completion announcement. The gate sits before the runtime branch, so it covers both the `subagent` and `acp` runtimes with one check.
4. **Completion-announce detection is a shared predicate.** `isCompletionAnnounceInputProvenance` (`src/sessions/input-provenance.ts`) covers `subagent_announce` plus the agent-mediated completion sources (`agent_harness_task`, media generators) and deliberately excludes `subagent_interrupted_resume`, where resuming the work is the task itself.

## Coverage matrix (per review)

| Surface | Status |
|---|---|
| Embedded runtime (`sessions_spawn`, subagent + ACP runtimes) | Mechanically enforced, unit-tested |
| CLI / MCP harnesses | Covered by the content-level evidence guard in the announcement itself |
| Codex-native `spawn_agent` | Owned by the `codex-rs` tool registry (`multi_agents_spec.rs`); not exposed through this repo's spawn path. Pending your scoping preference I will either open a `codex-rs` follow-up or extend the control here. |

## Tests

- `sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts`: the gate forbids fallback+announce turns on both runtimes, and allows fallback-only and announce-only turns.
- `input-provenance.test.ts`: predicate coverage including the resume exclusion.
- `model-fallback.probe.test.ts`: assertions updated for the fallback bit; announce and live-model-switch suites pass unchanged.
- `pnpm tsgo` and `pnpm tsgo:test` are clean locally.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** A fallback model serving a turn triggered by an internal completion announcement could convert the announced report into new spawned work (duplicate commits / re-executed tasks), as documented in #92271.

**Real environment tested:** Real OpenClaw runtime source from this branch executed on Linux (WSL2 Ubuntu, Node 22) via tsx against a freshly initialized isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME`. No mocks; the real `sessions_spawn` tool and real spawn machinery run end to end. The proof driver script lives outside the repo so no test scaffolding is committed to the branch.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
cd <branch-checkout> && pnpm exec tsx /home/<user>/openclaw-pr92011-proof.mts
# driver: creates an isolated OPENCLAW_HOME, builds the real sessions_spawn tool via
# createSessionsSpawnTool(...) once with { isFallbackRun: true, completionAnnounceTriggered: true }
# (incident shape) and once without (normal turn), and executes the same spawn request on both.
```

**Evidence after fix:** Copied live console output from the run above.

Scenario A — fallback turn + completion announcement (the #92271 incident shape):

```text
=== A: fallback turn + completion announcement (must be forbidden) ===
{
  "status": "forbidden",
  "error": "sessions_spawn is not allowed in this turn: a fallback model is replying to an internal completion announcement. The announced result is a report of already-completed work — summarize it for the user instead of re-executing or re-delegating it. If new work is genuinely required, wait for an explicit user instruction."
}
```

Scenario B — identical request on a normal turn (gate must not trip; request reaches the real spawn machinery, creating a real child session key and run id, then stops only at gateway credentials in the bare proof environment):

```text
=== B: normal turn, same request (gate must not trip) ===
{
  "status": "error",
  "error": "gateway agent requires credentials before opening a websocket\nFix: configure gateway.auth token/password, pair this device, or pass --token/--password.\nConfig: /tmp/openclaw-proof-PjbSSB/.openclaw/openclaw.json",
  "childSessionKey": "agent:main:subagent:95390638-9bac-4529-ae14-4f913e9af288",
  "runId": "f690c275-cb30-4afd-8680-b23355e8af2d"
}
```

**Observed result after fix:** The incident-shaped turn is refused with a `forbidden` status and an instructive message, while the identical request on a normal turn passes the gate and proceeds into the real spawn machinery — no false positive.

**Before evidence:** Documented in #92271 from a real production setup: redacted session-log and task-registry evidence showing a fallback turn spawning depth-1 duplicate tasks (including the verbatim task text "I am passing this task because I lack the terminal execution tool") and pushing a junk commit onto an open PR branch.

**What was not tested:** A live end-to-end provider-quota exhaustion forcing a real mid-session fallback (requires burning provider quota and a full gateway/channel setup); the fallback bit itself is covered by `model-fallback.probe.test.ts`. Codex-native `spawn_agent` is outside this repo's spawn path (see coverage matrix).

Fixes #92271

target.title: fix(daemon): detect and resolve dueling user+system systemd gateway units (#79375)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #79375 — on Linux, after an upgrade a **user-scope** unit
(`~/.config/systemd/user/openclaw-gateway.service`) and a **system-scope** unit
(`/etc/systemd/system/openclaw-gateway.service`) can both exist and both try to
manage the gateway. They bind the same port and each instance's stale-process
detection SIGTERMs the other, producing an endless restart cascade.

Root cause: `findInstalledSystemdGatewayScope` checked the user path first and
**returned early**, so it could never surface that both scopes coexist — the
"dueling" condition was structurally invisible.

## What changed (three layers)

**1. Detection (`src/daemon/systemd.ts`)**
- New `findSystemdGatewayInstallation(env)` returns a discriminated union
  `{ kind: "none" | "user" | "system" | "dueling" }` reporting *every* installed
  scope without early-returning. Dueling requires **the same canonical unit
  name** in both scopes — a custom marker-owned system unit (an intentional
  separate gateway, e.g. a rescue bot) is **not** treated as a duplicate, so a
  legitimate user gateway is never auto-removed.
- `findInstalledSystemdGatewayScope` delegates to it while **preserving the
  exact user-first preference** its four lifecycle callers rely on (no behavior
  change for stop/restart/is-enabled/runtime).
- New `uninstallUserSystemdGatewayUnit` removes only the `$HOME` user unit (no
  root), mirroring `uninstallLegacySystemdUnits`. New pure
  `formatDuelingScopesWarning` helper.

**2. Doctor resolution (`src/commands/doctor-gateway-services.ts`)**
- `maybeResolveDuelingSystemdGatewayScopes` detects the dueling state and, after
  the existing confirm/policy gate, removes the redundant **user-scope** unit
  while keeping the root-installed **system-scope** unit authoritative — exactly
  the reporter's manual workaround. Declining or an externally-managed policy
  falls back to the existing cleanup hints. Wired into the doctor health flow.

**3. Startup guard (`src/cli/gateway-cli/run.ts`)**
- In service mode, only when a stale-kill actually happened, log a targeted
  remediation pointing at `openclaw doctor --fix`. Diagnostic only — the kill
  decision is unchanged; the lookup is skipped on clean starts.

The system-scope unit is never auto-removed (needs root); only a `sudo` hint is
offered for that direction. No new config keys.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** On Linux a user-scope and a system-scope
`openclaw-gateway.service` coexisting after upgrade bind the same port and
SIGTERM each other in an endless restart loop (#79375). After this patch the
doctor resolution path detects the dueling pair and removes the redundant
user-scope unit while keeping the system-scope unit, breaking the loop; a
custom-named system gateway is left untouched.

**Real environment tested:** Linux (kernel 6.17, Ubuntu 24.04), Node v26.0.0,
pnpm 11.2.2, real `systemd` with a root-created unit under
`/etc/systemd/system`. To avoid touching the host's production gateway the proof
is isolated via `OPENCLAW_SYSTEMD_UNIT=openclaw-proof-gateway`; the real
`openclaw-gateway` service stayed `active` throughout (verified after).

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```bash
# 1. build the patched tree
NODE_OPTIONS="--max-old-space-size=8192" npx tsdown

# 2. create a REAL root-owned system unit (isolated name, never enabled/started)
sudo tee /etc/systemd/system/openclaw-proof-gateway.service <<'UNIT'
[Unit]
Description=OpenClaw Proof (isolated)
[Service]
ExecStart=/usr/bin/node x gateway --port 18789
UNIT

# 3. drive the COMPILED detector + the COMPILED user-unit uninstaller
#    (the same primitive doctor --fix calls) against real on-disk units
node /tmp/real-systemd-proof.mjs

# 4. clean up the isolated stubs
sudo rm -f /etc/systemd/system/openclaw-proof-gateway.service
```

**Evidence after fix:** live `node`/`systemd` terminal output, redacted to the
isolated unit name:
```
[2] system unit present on disk: true
    detector -> {"kind":"dueling",
      "user":{"scope":"user","unitName":"openclaw-proof-gateway.service",
              "unitPath":".../.config/systemd/user/openclaw-proof-gateway.service"},
      "system":{"scope":"system","unitName":"openclaw-proof-gateway.service",
              "unitPath":"/etc/systemd/system/openclaw-proof-gateway.service"}}
    warning  -> detected BOTH a user-scope (...) and a system-scope (...) gateway
       unit bound to port 18789; they will SIGTERM each other in a restart loop.
       Run `openclaw doctor --fix` to remove the redundant user-scope unit, or:
       systemctl --user disable --now openclaw-proof-gateway.service && rm .../...
[3] running real uninstallUserSystemdGatewayUnit (user scope, no root):
    Removed user-scope systemd service: .../systemd/user/openclaw-proof-gateway.service
    result -> {"unitName":"openclaw-proof-gateway.service", ..., "removed":true}
    user unit still on disk?  false
    system unit still on disk? true
[4] post-cleanup detector -> {"kind":"system", ...}
```

**Observed result after fix:** the detector returns `dueling` only when both
scopes hold the **same canonical** unit; the real uninstaller removed exactly
the user-scope unit on disk and left the root-owned system unit in place, so a
subsequent detection reports `system` (loop resolved). The host's production
`openclaw-gateway.service` remained `active` the whole time. (Supplemental:
`npx tsdown` build complete; `oxlint` exit 0; `vitest` 132 passed incl. the
"custom marker-owned system gateway is not dueling" P1 safety test.)

**What was not tested:** a full interactive `openclaw doctor --fix` session
end-to-end (prompt → confirm → reinstall) against the **production** gateway, to
avoid disturbing the live service; instead the exact compiled resolution
primitive it invokes (`uninstallUserSystemdGatewayUnit`) was exercised against
real isolated systemd units, and the doctor flow wiring is covered by unit tests
(`maybeResolveDuelingSystemdGatewayScopes`: confirm / decline / external-policy /
single-scope / non-Linux). The WSL2-specific restart loop from the original
report was not live-reproduced.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: fix(codex): strengthen agent soul adherence
target.body:
## Summary

- Strengthen the existing Codex app-server `AGENT_SOUL` preamble in one line.
- Name the failure mode this is meant to reduce: after longer interactions, persona and collaboration traits from workspace soul files can fade and the model can drift back toward generic assistant behavior.
- Keep the scope surgical: no file-purpose legend, no per-turn/loading wording, no redundant developer wording, and no test-only assertions.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Native Codex app-server turns now tell the model to treat `<AGENT_SOUL>` as authoritative context for identity, communication, work style, and the human relationship, reducing ambiguity when later user messages or long interactions pull toward generic GPT behavior.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, branch `fix/codex-agent-soul-preamble`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm test extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.test.ts`; `pnpm prompt:snapshots:check`; `git diff --check`; inspected sibling Codex source under `../codex/codex-rs/app-server/src/request_processors/turn_processor.rs` and `../codex/codex-rs/core/src/session/mod.rs` for collaboration-mode prompt handling.

Evidence after fix: Focused Codex app-server tests passed: 2 files, 138 tests. Prompt snapshots are current. Diff check is clean. Final PR diff is one production line in `extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-context.ts`.

Observed result after fix: The final diff only replaces the existing `AGENT_SOUL` preamble sentence; it no longer contains `File purposes:`, an `Apply these instructions on every turn` line, or redundant developer wording.

What was not tested: No live Codex app-server/Gateway run and no deployment.

## Verification

- `pnpm test extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.test.ts`
- `pnpm prompt:snapshots:check`
- `git diff --check`

target.title: fix: tune diagnostic liveness and idle queue depth
target.body:
## Summary
- add a separate 10s max event-loop delay threshold while keeping sustained P99 delay warnings at 1s
- clear diagnostic session `queueDepth` immediately when a session transitions to idle
- add focused regression coverage for both diagnostic behaviors

## Why
The diagnostic liveness sampler currently treats transient max event-loop spikes the same as sustained P99 delay. That makes short, isolated spikes noisy even when P99 remains healthy. The queue-depth tracker also decrements by one on idle, which can leave stale queued work visible after a session has completed.

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Diagnostic liveness warnings should not treat a transient max event-loop spike the same as sustained P99 delay, and completed/idle sessions should not retain stale queued work.
- **Real environment tested:** Local Windows OpenClaw Gateway install on `Zephyrus`, OpenClaw `2026.5.7 (eeef486)`, running as the active Telegram/Gateway setup.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Verified the active installed diagnostic bundle in the real local OpenClaw installation after applying the equivalent diagnostic change locally:
  - `openclaw --version`
  - `openclaw status --json`
  - `Select-String` against `C:\Users\kl_aj\AppData\Roaming\npm\node_modules\openclaw\dist\diagnostic-DbrcADVi.js` for `DEFAULT_LIVENESS_EVENT_LOOP_DELAY_MAX_WARN_MS`, the event-loop delay condition, and idle queue-depth handling.
- **Evidence after fix:** Copied live terminal output from the local OpenClaw setup:

```text
OpenClaw 2026.5.7 (eeef486)

openclaw status --json excerpt:
{
  "runtimeVersion": "2026.5.7",
  "tasks": {
    "total": 253,
    "active": 0,
    "terminal": 253,
    "failures": 0
  },
  "sessions": {
    "count": 27,
    "defaults": {
      "model": "gpt-5.5",
      "contextTokens": 200000
    }
  }
}

Select-String live output from active installed bundle:
file=C:\Users\kl_aj\AppData\Roaming\npm\node_modules\openclaw\dist\diagnostic-DbrcADVi.js
489:const DEFAULT_LIVENESS_EVENT_LOOP_DELAY_MAX_WARN_MS = 10e3;
619:if (eventLoopDelayP99Ms >= DEFAULT_LIVENESS_EVENT_LOOP_DELAY_WARN_MS || eventLoopDelayMaxMs >= DEFAULT_LIVENESS_EVENT_LOOP_DELAY_MAX_WARN_MS) reasons.push("event_loop_delay");
788:if (params.state === "idle") state.queueDepth = 0;
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The active real OpenClaw install contains the separate 10s max-delay threshold and clears `queueDepth` to `0` immediately on idle. The gateway/status command still responds from the local runtime after the diagnostic change is present.
- **What was not tested:** I did not force a real production 10s event-loop stall; the threshold behavior is additionally covered by focused tests in this PR.

## Test plan
- `pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.logging.config.ts src/logging/diagnostic.test.ts` — 38 passed
- `pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.logging.config.ts src/logging/diagnostic.test.ts --testNamePattern "clears queued work|ignores transient event-loop max spikes|warns for transient event-loop max spikes above|warns for sustained P99"` — 4 passed
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/logging/diagnostic.ts src/logging/diagnostic.test.ts` — 0 warnings, 0 errors
- `$env:NODE_OPTIONS='--max-old-space-size=8192'; pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false -p tsconfig.core.json` — passed

Note: a full `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false --skipLibCheck false -p tsconfig.json` run hit Node heap OOM locally before the narrower core typecheck passed with an 8GB heap.

target.title: fix(googlechat): preserve thread reply target through delivery
target.body: ## Summary - **Problem:** Google Chat replies could leave the inbound Chat thread and appear as new top-level messages. - **Why it matters:** delayed or tool-driven assistant replies break group-thread UX when they detach from the conversation the user spoke into. - **What changed:** preserve `message.thread.name` as `MessageThreadId`, expose it to reply/message-tool threading, and normalize Google Chat outbound thread targets before they reach `thread.name`. - **What did NOT change:** no Google Chat per-thread OpenClaw session isolation; core reply plumbing remains channel-agnostic and Google Chat resource-name handling stays behind the Google Chat boundary. ## Change Type (select all) - [x] Bug fix - [ ] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [ ] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [x] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [x] Integrations - [x] API / contracts - [ ] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Related #69422 - Related #39554 - [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs) - Behavior or issue addressed: Google Chat assistant replies from existing Chat threads should remain in the inbound thread. - Real environment tested: Linux VPS-hosted OpenClaw Google Chat runtime with service-account auth and a real Google Chat space. - Exact steps or command run after this patch: Jai sent real Google Chat threaded messages and confirmed assistant replies stayed in-thread before this PR was marked ready. - Evidence after fix: screenshot evidence is attached below: - Before: Google Chat reply appeared outside the inbound thread. - After: Google Chat reply stayed inside the inbound thread. - Scope note: typing-placeholder editing is separate follow-up behavior; this PR fixes thread placement. - Observed result after fix: live Google Chat replies stayed in-thread; local seam tests also verified natural replies, message-tool sends, and current-reply behavior use the inbound Google Chat thread resource. - What was not tested: every Google Chat account/space policy combination; typing-placeholder edit/cleanup for message-tool sends; Google Chat per-thread OpenClaw session isolation, which is out of scope. - Before evidence: prior behavior re-rooted replies as new top-level Chat messages. ## Root Cause (if applicable) - Root cause: Google Chat exposes different resource shapes for messages (`spaces/.../messages/...`) and threads (`spaces/.../threads/...`), and OpenClaw previously let message-shaped values flow into thread-routing seams. - Missing detection / guardrail: tests covered legacy single-resource channels, but not a provider where current message id and current thread id are different resource names. - Contributing context: - Inbound webhook context did not populate generic `MessageThreadId` from `message.thread.name`. - The message-tool path had no inbound-thread default when the agent omitted `threadId`. - `[[reply_to_current]]` resolves to the current message id; for Google Chat that value must be converted to the inbound thread before delivery. ## Regression Test Plan (if applicable) - Coverage level that should have caught this: - [x] Unit test - [x] Seam / integration test - [ ] End-to-end test - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient - Target test or file: - `src/auto-reply/reply/reply-plumbing.test.ts` - `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-payloads.test.ts` - `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-utils.test.ts` - `src/auto-reply/reply/reply-delivery.test.ts` - `extensions/googlechat/src/monitor.webhook-routing.test.ts` - `extensions/googlechat/src/monitor.reply-delivery.test.ts` - `extensions/googlechat/src/channel.test.ts` - `extensions/googlechat/src/actions.test.ts` - `extensions/googlechat/src/targets.test.ts` - Scenario the test should lock in: Google Chat has a current message resource and a distinct current thread resource; implicit replies and message-tool sends use the thread resource, while message-shaped reply ids are rewritten or rejected before reaching the Chat API. - Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it covers webhook ingress, core reply seams, message-tool routing, channel delivery, and the API boundary without requiring live Google Chat in CI. - Existing test that already covers this (if any): none. - If no new test is added, why not: N/A. ## User-visible / Behavior Changes Google Chat assistant replies from an existing Chat thread now stay in that thread instead of creating a new top-level message. ## Diagram (if applicable) ```text Before: Google Chat webhook -> currentMessageId=spaces/.../messages/... -> delivery sends that as thread.name -> Chat API rejects/falls back -> new top-level thread After: Google Chat webhook -> MessageThreadId=spaces/.../threads/... -> reply/tool delivery resolves outbound thread -> Chat API receives thread.name=spaces/.../threads/... ``` ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? `No` - Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No` - New/changed network calls? `No` - Command/tool execution surface changed? `No` - Data access scope changed? `No` - If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - OS: macOS local checkout; Linux VPS-hosted runtime for real Chat verification - Runtime/container: OpenClaw source checkout; local verification on exact rebased head `a617644c81dd43bb7285d3001ca6d19750e62e68`; live Google Chat verification on a Linux VPS-hosted runtime with the thread-placement patch applied - Model/provider: N/A - Integration/channel (if any): Google Chat service-account auth - Relevant config (redacted): Google Chat enabled with group mention policy; no secret/config changes required ### Steps 1. Send a threaded Google Chat message that mentions the bot. 2. Let the assistant produce a normal final reply. 3. Exercise message-tool sends without explicit `threadId`. 4. Exercise `[[reply_to_current]]` behavior where the core seam supplies the current message id. ### Expected - Replies and message-tool sends stay in the original Google Chat thread. - Explicit thread-shaped ids are preserved. - Message-shaped or malformed thread targets never reach `thread.name` as-is. ### Actual - Before this patch: replies could re-root as new top-level Chat messages. - After this patch: live verification and local seam coverage keep replies in the inbound thread. - Scope note: typing-placeholder editing is separate follow-up behavior and is not fixed by this PR. ## Evidence - [x] Failing test/log before + passing after - [ ] Trace/log snippets - [x] Screenshot/recording - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant) Screenshot evidence attached below: - Before screenshot: Google Chat reply escaped the inbound thread and appeared outside the original conversation thread. - After screenshot: Google Chat reply stayed inside the inbound thread. - Scope note: the after screenshot may still show a stale typing placeholder when the message-tool path is used; typing-placeholder edit/cleanup is not part of this PR. node@22.22.2 -- ./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md extensions/googlechat/src/actions.test.ts extensions/googlechat/src/actions.ts extensions/googlechat/src/api.ts target.title: fix(qqbot): add TCP keepalive + heartbeat interval logging to prevent silent connection drops
target.body:
### Problem

**Messages delayed by up to 9 minutes due to silent WebSocket connection failures.**

On 2026-05-04 07:13, a user sent a message via QQBot. The message was not received until 07:22:18 — **9 minutes of delay**. During this entire period, the OpenClaw gateway showed no errors, no reconnection attempts, and no heartbeat failures. The connection appeared healthy but was in fact dead.

**What actually happened:**
- The QQ WebSocket connection was open from 07:04:03 to 07:22:16 (18 minutes, no drops logged)
- At 07:22:16, the connection abruptly closed with code `1006` (abnormal close, no close frame)
- Immediately after reconnecting at 07:22:18, all queued messages arrived at once
- **Conclusion:** The connection silently died well before 07:13, but neither the client nor the QQ server detected it. Messages were buffered server-side and only delivered after the client reconnect.

**Why no detection?** The existing QQBot gateway implementation has two gaps:
1. **No TCP keepalive** — The `ws` library was instantiated without `setKeepAlive`, meaning the OS-level TCP connection is not probed. Network middleboxes (routers, NAT, firewalls) silently drop idle connections without sending RST/FIN.
2. **No heartbeat timeout detection** — If the server sends a `HEARTBEAT_ACK` on time, the client assumes the connection is healthy. But if the `HEARTBEAT_ACK` is lost in transit (because the underlying TCP path is dead), the client never notices. There is no timeout or ACK validation.

**Root cause:** The 1006 abnormal close indicates the connection was terminated without a proper WebSocket close frame — likely at the TCP or network layer (NAT timeout, silent packet drop). The client had no way to detect this before receiving a message, because there was no lower-level keepalive probe and no heartbeat ACK timeout.

### Solution

**Three changes in `extensions/qqbot/src/engine/gateway/gateway-connection.ts`:**

#### 1. TCP Keepalive (Prevention)
```typescript
const socket = (ws as unknown as { _socket: Socket | null })._socket;
if (socket) {
  socket.setKeepAlive(true, 30_000);  // probe every 30s
  socket.setTimeout(60_000);          // 60s with no data → timeout
  socket.on("timeout", () => {
    log?.warn?.(`WebSocket socket timeout — closing and reconnecting`);
    ws.close();  // triggers existing reconnect flow
  });
}
```

- `setKeepAlive(true, 30_000)` — OS sends TCP keepalive probes every 30 seconds, keeping NAT mappings alive and detecting dead connections at the transport layer
- `setTimeout(60_000)` — if no data (including keepalive ACK) is received for 60 seconds, the socket is considered dead and closed, triggering an immediate reconnect

#### 2. Heartbeat Interval Logging (Debugging)
```typescript
const interval = d.heartbeat_interval;
this.ctx.log?.info(`Heartbeat interval: ${interval}ms (${(interval / 1000).toFixed(1)}s)`);
```

- Logs the heartbeat interval sent by the QQ server on every connect/resume
- Previously unknown: QQ server uses **41.25 seconds**, not a round number
- Critical for diagnosing heartbeat-related issues in the future

#### 3. WebSocket Compression
```typescript
perMessageDeflate: true
```
- Standard best practice for `ws` library; reduces bandwidth for JSON payloads

### Verification

- **7 new unit tests** for keepalive and heartbeat logging logic
- **50 existing tests** pass (message-queue, outbound-dispatch, inbound-pipeline, envelope-stage)
- **Live deployment test:** After deploying, TCP socket timeout was correctly detected on a dead connection at 10:38:48, triggering automatic reconnect. Heartbeat interval logged as `41250ms (41.3s)`.

### Expected Impact

- **Before:** Dead connections go undetected for minutes. Users see message delays of 5-10 minutes. Only a manual restart or the next server-side close triggers reconnect.
- **After:** Dead connections detected within 60 seconds via TCP keepalive timeout. Automatic reconnect restores message delivery. Heartbeat interval visible in logs for troubleshooting.

target.title: docs(prometheus): warn that plugins.allow is strict-mode
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a `<Warning>` callout to the Prometheus Quick Start clarifying that `plugins.allow` is **strict-mode** — when set, only listed plugins are eligible to load. The current Quick Start example shows `allow: ["diagnostics-prometheus"]` without warning that this disables every other configured plugin on a real running gateway.

## AI assistance disclosure

This PR was drafted with **Claude Code** (Anthropic's CLI agent).

- [x] **AI-assisted** — drafted by Claude Code (this section makes it explicit; trailing footer also marks the agent)
- [x] **Testing**: lightly tested. Empirically reproduced the failure mode that motivates this Warning — `plugins.allow: ["diagnostics-prometheus"]` dropped a 72-plugin running v2026.5.2 gateway to 9 enabled plugins; recovered via `openclaw config unset plugins.allow` + gateway restart. Did NOT build the docs site locally — render check left for CI / maintainer.
- [x] **Author understanding** — confirmed source-level grasp of the activation flow (`src/plugins/config-activation-shared.ts` `resolvePluginActivationDecisionShared`), the three narrow exceptions to `plugins.allow` (`selected-memory-slot`, `selected-context-engine-slot`, conditional `bundled-channel-enabled-in-config`), and the capability gate at `services-CTQW_M_S.js:15` that motivated cross-referenced #76628.
- [ ] **`codex review --base origin/main` not run locally** — Codex CLI is not installed in this contributor environment. Relying on the GitHub Codex bot (clawsweeper) auto-review run that posted to this PR as the AI review pass.
- [x] **Bot review conversations** — clawsweeper Codex review on this PR addressed in a follow-up comment below (covers the soft suggestion about example tuning).

## Why

Following the Quick Start example as-written on a multi-plugin gateway (e.g. one running `memory-core`, `active-memory`, `memory-wiki`, `telegram`, etc.) silently flips the gateway into strict-allowlist mode and drops all other configured plugins. On a 71-plugin running gateway (mid-sized self-hosted setup), the example dropped the enabled-plugin count from 72 to **9** — including critical plugins like `memory-core`, `active-memory`, and the configured Telegram channel — until rolled back via `openclaw config unset plugins.allow`.

The warning surface for this is misleading in two ways:

1. The startup log line `[plugins] plugins.allow is empty; discovered non-bundled plugins may auto-load: <id>. Set plugins.allow to explicit trusted ids.` *implies* setting `plugins.allow` is the right next step. It isn't, in the general case.
2. The schema description for `plugins.allow` is correct (*"when set, only listed plugins are eligible to load"*), but the Quick Start snippet doesn't reproduce that semantics in user-visible documentation.

This PR adds a single `<Warning>` callout next to the snippet, naming the failure mode explicitly so users on existing multi-plugin gateways don't trip the regression.

## Cross-references

- **#75575** (open) — *Honor `plugins.allow` on runtime provider paths (currently force-loaded) — propose `plugins.bundledMode` opt-in*. Same family of `plugins.allow` semantic confusion. The Warning links to that issue for users who want the longer architectural discussion.
- **#76628** (just filed) — *@openclaw/diagnostics-prometheus loads via npm but internalDiagnostics capability is bundled-only* — describes a separate but adjacent failure mode in the same Quick Start path; mentions this PR's `plugins.allow` finding as one of the empirical investigations along the way.

## Scope

- Single-file doc change: `docs/gateway/prometheus.md`
- No code changes
- No behavior changes
- Backward compatible (callouts are additive)

## Test plan

- [x] Source-level check: `<Warning>` is the same MDX component already used elsewhere in the same file (`docs/gateway/prometheus.md:19-21`), so no new component dependencies. Indentation matches the surrounding `<Step>` / `<Tabs>` block.
- [ ] Docs preview render — left for maintainer/CI; I have not built the docs site locally.
- [x] Empirically reproduced the regression that this Warning describes (multi-plugin gateway dropping from 72 → 9 enabled plugins after applying the existing snippet, fully recovered by `openclaw config unset plugins.allow` + restart). See **#76628** for the full reproduction.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: fix(agents): pause yielded main-session runs
target.body:
## Summary

Extend the subagent yield-pause fix from [6f3b5f86](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/6f3b5f8666f3a3877b62d9019b8842455fdf62e1) to top-level (non-subagent) sessions.

When a main session ends via the `sessions_yield` tool, the runner already emits a lifecycle event with `yielded: true` and `livenessState: "paused"`. The runner's terminal lifecycle metadata, the agent-job snapshot cache, and the agent.wait dedupe entry all propagate those flags generically, so subagent and non-subagent runs benefit equally from those parts of 6f3b5f86.

The gap is in `gateway/session-lifecycle-state.ts`. `deriveGatewaySessionLifecycleSnapshot` does not look at the `yielded` flag, so the session entry is persisted with `status: \"done\"` even though the runner has queued a post-yield continuation that drains on the next turn. Dashboards, restart recovery, and follow-up turns then treat the session as finished and race the continuation, producing the in-context overflow and out-of-order events the user reports.

## Why the same fix needs to apply to the top-level session

The subagent registry tracks active subagent runs in memory and uses `pauseReason: \"sessions_yield\"` to keep yielded entries in a paused state instead of marking them complete. The main session has no equivalent in-memory registry; its lifecycle state is persisted directly through `persistGatewaySessionLifecycleEvent` into the on-disk session entry. The gap shows up in three places:

1. The persisted session entry says `status: \"done\"`, so `sessions_list`, `sessions.changed` broadcasts, and dashboard UI all show the session as finished.
2. `main-session-restart-recovery` is keyed off `entry.abortedLastRun` and `entry.status === \"running\"`. With status=done it leaves the entry alone, but on a clean restart-mid-yield the yielded session can also end up `abortedLastRun=true` (the cleaned-lock path), at which point recovery would force a `[System] Your previous turn was interrupted...` resume that races the queued continuation.
3. A new user message arrives at a session that is internally paused but externally \"done\" — the gateway dispatches a fresh agent.run, and the queued steered yield-interrupt drains alongside the new user input, producing out-of-order assistant text.

## Shape of the change

- Recognize the lifecycle `yielded` flag in `deriveGatewaySessionLifecycleSnapshot` and derive `status: \"paused\"` with `pauseReason: \"sessions_yield\"` instead of `done`. Yielded short-circuits before the `aborted`/`stopReason` branches so partial classifications cannot mask the paused state.
- Clear `pauseReason` on the next lifecycle `start` event so a fresh run reactivates the session, mirroring how `subagent-registry-lifecycle.ts` clears `pauseReason` on completion.
- Add `\"paused\"` to `SessionRunStatus` in both `gateway/session-utils.types.ts` and `agents/tools/sessions-helpers.ts`, and add `pauseReason?: \"sessions_yield\"` to `SessionEntry` and `GatewaySessionRow`.
- Surface `pauseReason` on the `sessions.changed` broadcast snapshot and the `loadGatewaySessionRow` builder so consumers (UI, sessions_list tool) see the paused state.
- Tighten `isTerminalSessionStatus` so the type predicate stays accurate after \"paused\" joins the union (paused is not terminal).
- Skip `main-session-restart-recovery` for entries with `pauseReason: \"sessions_yield\"` so they are not re-prompted with the generic interrupted-turn message.

The runner-level changes (yieldDetected -> livenessState=paused/yielded=true) and the gateway agent-job/agent-wait-dedupe propagation that 6f3b5f86 already added are unchanged. This PR plugs the missing seam between those generic signals and the persisted session entry.

## Test coverage

- `src/gateway/session-lifecycle-state.test.ts`:
  - `marks yielded lifecycle end events as paused with sessions_yield reason` (persisted patch).
  - `treats yielded as paused even when aborted is set` (yield short-circuit).
  - `clears pauseReason when a fresh run starts on a paused session` (start-phase reactivation).
  - Existing terminal-mapping tests updated to include explicit `pauseReason: undefined` on non-yield ends.
- `src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts`:
  - `skips sessions paused via sessions_yield instead of forcing a resume`.
  - `does not mark sessions with sessions_yield pauseReason as restart-aborted from cleaned locks`.

## Local validation

- `pnpm tsgo:core`, `pnpm tsgo:core:test`, `pnpm tsgo:extensions`, `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test` — clean.
- `pnpm test src/gateway/session-lifecycle-state.test.ts src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts src/gateway/server-chat.agent-events.test.ts src/config/sessions.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-list-tool.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.create-event.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent-wait-dedupe.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts src/agents/subagent-announce-output.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/sessions-yield.orchestration.test.ts` — all green (235 tests).
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check`, `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs`, `pnpm format:docs:check`, `pnpm check:changelog-attributions` — all clean on changed files.

## Test plan

- [ ] CI green on \`fix(agents): pause yielded main-session runs\`.
- [ ] Manual smoke: trigger `sessions_yield` from a main-session conversation; confirm the session entry persists `status: paused` and `pauseReason: sessions_yield`, and that a follow-up turn flips back to `running` with `pauseReason: undefined`.
- [ ] Restart-recovery smoke: kill the gateway while a main session is paused via yield; confirm restart-recovery skips it instead of re-prompting with the interrupted-turn message.


## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: Before this PR, when a top-level (non-subagent) embedded `openclaw` run ended via the `sessions_yield` tool, the runner emitted `yielded=true` and `livenessState=paused` but the gateway treated it as a clean completion and wrote `status=done`. The session displayed as finished while awaiting a queued post-yield continuation, racing dashboards, restart-recovery, and follow-up turns. After this PR, the lifecycle persistence derives `status=paused` with `pauseReason=sessions_yield`, restart-recovery skips paused sessions, and `SessionRunStatus` adds `"paused"` so consumers can distinguish a yielded run from a completed one.
- **Real environment tested**: live OpenClaw gateway on rh-bot.lan, pid 65734, build SHA 23804e610c (upgrade-v2026.5.28 with this PR cherry-picked). The deployed `dist/server-chat-*.js` and `dist/main-session-restart-recovery-*.js` chunks carry the new `pauseReason`/`sessions_yield` logic; the proof script imports and executes those exact bundle bytes.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```bash
  # On rh-bot.lan, confirm deployed bundles carry the PR logic:
  grep -c 'sessions_yield\|pauseReason' \
    /Users/alexm/.openclaw/openclaw/dist/main-session-restart-recovery-CirHJY3p.js
  # -> 1 (the new shouldSkipMainRecovery branch)

  grep -c 'sessions_yield\|pauseReason' \
    /Users/alexm/.openclaw/openclaw/dist/server-chat-D9hpq-gf.js
  # -> non-zero (new deriveGatewaySessionLifecycleSnapshot branches)

  # Drive the deployed code with three scenarios:
  node /tmp/proof-75662-amittell.mjs
  ```
  Proof script (`/tmp/proof-75662-amittell.mjs`) extracts the deployed
  `deriveGatewaySessionLifecycleSnapshot` from `server-chat-D9hpq-gf.js` via
  `vm.runInContext`, dynamically imports `main-session-restart-recovery-CirHJY3p.js`
  to grab the exported `markRestartAbortedMainSessions`, seeds a temp
  state-dir with one yielded and one non-yielded `running` entry, and asserts
  three behaviors:
  1. Lifecycle `phase=end` with `yielded=true` -> `status=paused`,
     `pauseReason=sessions_yield`, `abortedLastRun=false`.
  2. Same entry passed to restart-recovery is SKIPPED (no `abortedLastRun`
     write, `updatedAt` unchanged).
  3. Sibling running entry without `pauseReason` is marked
     `abortedLastRun=true` (existing behavior preserved).
  Plus a guard check: lifecycle `phase=error` with `yielded=true` returns
  `status=failed` with NO `pauseReason` leak.
- **Evidence after fix**:
  ```
  === PR #75662 real-behavior proof ===
  host=rh-bot.lan pid=84884 node=v25.8.2
  dist=/Users/alexm/.openclaw/openclaw/dist

  [1] lifecycle bundle: /Users/alexm/.openclaw/openclaw/dist/server-chat-D9hpq-gf.js
  [1a] yielded terminal -> {"updatedAt":1780285520789,"status":"paused","startedAt":1780285519789,"endedAt":1780285520789,"runtimeMs":1000,"abortedLastRun":false,"pauseReason":"sessions_yield"}
  [1a] OK: paused status carries pauseReason=sessions_yield
  [1b] non-yielded terminal -> {"updatedAt":1780285520789,"status":"done","startedAt":1780285519789,"endedAt":1780285520789,"runtimeMs":1000,"abortedLastRun":false}
  [1b] OK: non-yielded run preserves status=done with no pauseReason
  [1c] error+yielded -> {"updatedAt":1780285520789,"status":"failed","startedAt":1780285519789,"endedAt":1780285520789,"runtimeMs":1000,"abortedLastRun":false}
  [1c] OK: error phase masks yielded marker, no pauseReason leak

  [2] seeded /var/folders/cn/.../state/agents/main/sessions/sessions.json
  [2] recovery bundle: /Users/alexm/.openclaw/openclaw/dist/main-session-restart-recovery-CirHJY3p.js
  [2] exports: i, n, r, t
  [main-session-restart-recovery] marked 1 interrupted main session(s) for restart recovery (pr-75662-proof)
  [2] mark result: {"marked":1,"skipped":1}
  [2] yielded entry after: {"sessionId":"sess-yielded","status":"running","pauseReason":"sessions_yield","startedAt":...,"updatedAt":1780285519789}
  [2] normal entry after:  {"sessionId":"sess-normal","status":"running","startedAt":...,"updatedAt":1780285521266,"abortedLastRun":true}
  [2a] OK: yielded session was skipped (no abortedLastRun, no updatedAt bump)
  [2b] OK: non-yielded running session was marked abortedLastRun=true

  === ALL PROOFS PASSED ===
  ```
- **Observed result after fix**: All three scenarios pass on the deployed bundle. A yielded terminal event produces `status=paused`/`pauseReason=sessions_yield` and is skipped by restart recovery (no `abortedLastRun` mutation, no `updatedAt` bump). A non-yielded terminal event still produces `status=done` with no `pauseReason` (existing behavior preserved). An error phase masks the `yielded` flag and reports `status=failed` with no `pauseReason` leak, matching the gating comment in `resolveTerminalStatus`.
- **Targeted tests**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts src/gateway/session-lifecycle-state.test.ts` -> 22 passed + 15 passed (37/37). Broader: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts src/gateway/sessions-patch.test.ts src/gateway/session-message-events.test.ts` -> 172/172 passed. `node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --only=core --threads=4` -> clean.
- **What was not tested**: End-to-end live channel trigger (sending a real Telegram message that drives `sessions_yield` to completion); this is exercised by the existing vitest coverage in `pi-embedded-runner/sessions-yield.orchestration.test.ts` rather than reproduced live on rh-bot.


## Live exact-head capture (2026-06-12): found and fixed an emit-side gap

Running the requested live scenario on an isolated sandbox gateway (loopback :28795, fresh `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, Control UI webchat, native `openai-completions` lane with a tool-calling model) surfaced a real defect in this PR's original head: **the paused state never persisted in live runs, on any lane.** A store probe polling every 0.3s across four real `sessions_spawn` + `sessions_yield` turns at head `be96a422ff` captured the persisted row going `running -> killed` with `abortedLastRun: true` at yield time -- the exact pre-PR failure this PR exists to prevent.

**Root cause (probe-instrumented):** `sessions_yield` aborts the attempt mid-stream (`runAbortController.abort("sessions_yield")`), and the terminal lifecycle event can be emitted from the abort teardown before `run.ts` post-processing writes the `yielded` meta. The bundle-level instrumentation captured the failing ordering directly:

```
emitLifecycleTerminal state.yielded = undefined  aborted = false   <- event emitted first
meta.yielded set -> false                                          <- run.ts wrote 551ms later
```

The event reaches `deriveGatewaySessionLifecycleSnapshot` carrying `aborted` without `yielded`, and `resolveTerminalStatus` correctly resolves killed. The projection/recovery/UI logic this PR adds was never the problem; the emit side never fed it. The unit tests passed because they fabricate events that already carry `yielded: true`.

**Fix (in `a46ba508b8`):** `onYield` now records `yielded` through the subscription's terminal lifecycle meta channel at tool time, before the abort, using the same late-binding pattern the yield abort itself uses (`src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts`). A regression test pins the handler half: an end event with `state.yielded` set and `aborted` resolved from the `isTerminalAborted` fallback still carries `yielded` (`src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.lifecycle.test.ts`).

**Post-fix live proof, end to end (same sandbox, instrumented bundle):**

```
10:27:40.065  [PROBE] meta.yielded set -> true              (onYield records at tool time)
10:27:40.423  [PROBE] emitLifecycleTerminal state.yielded = true   (event carries it)
10:27:4x      store: status=paused pauseReason=sessions_yield abortedLastRun=false
~10:28        Sessions Control UI: main session "Paused", subagent "Live"
10:28:1x      gateway KILLED while paused -> store unchanged (paused, ab=false)
10:28:3x      gateway restarted -> recovery skips the paused session;
              no "[System] Your previous turn was interrupted" prompt in the chat
10:29:20      subagent (orphan-recovered post-restart) completes -> announce drains
              the queued continuation in order -> running, pauseReason cleared
10:30:48      continuation finishes -> done, abortedLastRun=false
```

Reproduced again on a second pass (store probe `paused/sessions_yield` at 15:54-15:56 with closure-identity probes confirming the onYield-time recording). This covers all three asks: visible Paused state, restart skip, and orderly continuation, captured live at the exact head plus the one-commit fix.

**Codex lane now covered too (was a sibling-lane gap, now fixed in this PR).** The Codex app-server harness lane emits its own terminal lifecycle event (`extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts` `emitLifecycleTerminal`) and was dropping the yield meta: the `phase:"end"` event carried only `phase`/`aborted`, never `yielded`, so the shared projection (`resolveYielded`) persisted Codex-backed yields as done/killed. The fix forwards `{yielded, livenessState:"paused", stopReason:"end_turn"}` on that event when `result.yieldDetected`, exactly as the native runner does, gated on the flag the `sessions_yield` tool already sets via `onYield -> onYieldDetected`. The Codex lifecycle event flows through the same `onAgentEvent -> server-chat -> persistGatewaySessionLifecycleEvent -> resolveYielded` path as the native lane.

Proven live on the Codex lane: a real `openai/gpt-5.5` turn through the Codex app-server (ChatGPT OAuth) that calls `sessions_yield` now persists `status=paused`, `pauseReason=sessions_yield` (was `done` pre-fix), and the subagent then drains the queued continuation in order back to `done`. An integration test (`extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.yield-lifecycle.test.ts`) asserts the Codex lifecycle end event carries `yielded:true`/`livenessState:"paused"`/`stopReason:"end_turn"` on yield and omits them on a normal turn (verified red without the fix).

Codex protocol behavior checked against the sibling `../codex` source (HEAD `b8aea2340`): `codex-rs/app-server-protocol/schema/typescript/ServerRequest.ts:19` defines the `{ "method": "item/tool/call", id, params: DynamicToolCallParams }` app-server -> client request, which is how the app-server invokes the OpenClaw `sessions_yield` dynamic tool (setting `yieldDetected`); turn lifecycle requests (`turn/start`/`turn/steer`/`turn/interrupt`) are at `ClientRequest.ts:90`. This change adds no new protocol dependency -- it forwards an OpenClaw-side flag onto OpenClaw's own lifecycle event.

target.title: fix(heartbeat): suppress no-op system event replies
target.body:
## Summary
- add centralized no-op sentinel detection for silent heartbeat/system-event replies
- suppress exact `HEARTBEAT_OK`, `NO_REPLY`, `NO_NEW_AUDIO`, and `SESSION_WATCHDOG_OK` outputs before delivery
- keep meaningful relayable exec summaries deliverable without moving cron events out of heartbeat-mode ack suppression
- preserve newer heartbeat prefix/model-selection behavior after rebasing onto current `origin/main`

Fixes #73149.

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Heartbeat/system-event handoffs could visibly deliver no-op sentinel replies such as exact `HEARTBEAT_OK`, while still needing to deliver meaningful exec/system-event summaries.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout for PR #73785 on Linux/Node 22 after rebasing onto current `origin/main` at `3de97057d0`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran `node --import tsx tmp/pr73785-runtime-proof.mjs` from the patched checkout. The script imported the patched `runHeartbeatOnce`, used the real in-process system-event queue, registered the channel plugin send path with outbound capture, seeded a real file-backed session store, and exercised both cron-event and exec-event paths.
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output copied from the after-fix proof command:

  ```json
  {
    "cron": {
      "name": "cron",
      "result": {
        "status": "ran",
        "durationMs": 27
      },
      "provider": "cron-event",
      "sent": []
    },
    "exec": {
      "name": "exec",
      "result": {
        "status": "ran",
        "durationMs": 1257
      },
      "provider": "exec-event",
      "sent": [
        {
          "to": "120363140186826074@g.us",
          "text": "Command completed: uploaded report.txt"
        }
      ]
    }
  }
  ```

- **Observed result after fix:** A cron-event reply of `HEARTBEAT_OK Watchdog clean` stayed in heartbeat-mode short-ack suppression and produced no outbound send. A meaningful exec-event reply still crossed the send boundary as `Command completed: uploaded report.txt`.
- **What was not tested:** No real WhatsApp or Telegram message was sent; the proof used a dummy WhatsApp group id and captured the outbound send locally to avoid test noise in real channels.

## Validation
- `CI=true pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/agents/model-runtime-policy.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.ts src/auto-reply/tokens.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.respects-ackmaxchars-heartbeat-acks.test.ts`

target.title: fix(memory): skip placeholder short-term promotions
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Closes #80582.

Short-term memory promotion could treat markdown and QMD scaffolding as durable content. That included empty list/checklist/fence/table fragments, placeholder daily-note headings, generic daily/dreaming headings, QMD hunk wrappers, collapsed persisted heading prefixes, and mixed snippets that carried structural text alongside real content.

## Why This Change Was Made

The fix centralizes short-term snippet promotability in the promotion resolver and applies that resolver across recall recording, store normalization, grounded candidates, ranking, relocation/rehydration, and direct promotion application.

Placeholder-only snippets are skipped. Mixed snippets keep real content while dropping structural wrappers such as QMD hunk headers, generic daily/dreaming headings, collapsed heading prefixes, and bullet-prefixed collapsed heading prefixes. Ordinary non-generic bullets remain intact.

The diagnostic skipped recall event now has an additive `unpromotable-short-term-snippet` reason, with the Plugin SDK API baseline hash regenerated for the exported event type change.

## User Impact

Memory promotion should carry actual remembered content instead of daily-note scaffolding or QMD placeholder text. Existing meaningful snippets still promote, and legacy event readers continue to read recorded recall events while diagnostic readers can see skipped recall records.

## Evidence

Duplicate check:

- `gh pr list --repo openclaw/openclaw --state open --search "80582 in:body" --json number,title,url,updatedAt,isDraft,author`
- Result: no open PRs found.

Commands run on the final rebased branch:

- `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD`
- `node --max-old-space-size=8192 --import tsx scripts/generate-plugin-sdk-api-baseline.ts --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/short-term-promotion.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/memory-host-events.test.ts`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

Results:

- Plugin SDK API baseline: OK.
- Vitest: 2 shards passed, 141 tests passed.
- Local autoreview: clean, no accepted/actionable findings.

Not run:

- Full `pnpm check`.
- Broad Testbox/Crabbox or live-channel proof.

AI-assisted: drafted and validated with Codex; human author should review and own the final PR before marking ready for review.

target.title: Fix: Compact inbound metadata
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

If this PR fixes a plugin beta-release blocker, title it `fix(<plugin-id>): beta blocker - <summary>` and link the matching `Beta blocker: <plugin-name> - <summary>` issue labeled `beta-blocker`. Contributors cannot label PRs, so the title is the PR-side signal for maintainers and automation.

  - Problem: Direct Telegram/chat messages can prepend repeated full Conversation info and Sender untrusted metadata JSON blocks before simple user text.
  - Why it matters: The repeated blocks add prompt noise and token overhead, and can make short direct-chat messages harder for the model to interpret.
  - What changed: Added optional messages.inboundMetadataMode: "compact-direct" to render direct-chat metadata as one compact untrusted line, while preserving full fenced metadata for groups and richer context.
  - What did NOT change (scope boundary): Defaults remain unchanged; group chats, replies, forwards, locations, structured context, and history keep the existing full metadata format.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [x] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #72704
- Related #62077
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

  - Root cause: Inbound prompt assembly always used the full untrusted metadata block format for direct external-channel messages.
  - Missing detection / guardrail: Existing tests covered safety and metadata presence, but not compact direct-chat prompt shape or stripping compact AI-facing metadata from user-visible history.
  - Contributing context (if known): Provider-level metadata fields are not portable across supported model providers, so the fix stays in prompt assembly/ config.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

For bug fixes or regressions, name the smallest reliable test coverage that should catch this. Otherwise write `N/A`.

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
  - Target test or file: src/auto-reply/reply/inbound-meta.test.ts, src/auto-reply/reply/strip-inbound-meta.test.ts, src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run.media- only.test.ts
  - Scenario the test should lock in: Compact direct metadata suppresses full sender/conversation JSON, group metadata stays full, config reaches prompt construction, and compact metadata is stripped from user-visible text.
  - Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: These tests cover the prompt builder, the call site, and the display/history sanitizer without requiring a live Telegram bot.
  - Existing test that already covers this (if any): Existing inbound metadata tests covered full metadata behavior and safety escaping.
  - If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

  New optional config: messages.inboundMetadataMode: "compact-direct".

  Default behavior remains unchanged.

## Diagram (if applicable)

For UI changes or non-trivial logic flows, include a small ASCII diagram reviewers can scan quickly. Otherwise write `N/A`.

```text
  Before:
  [direct chat message] -> [full conversation JSON + full sender JSON + body]

  After:
  [direct chat message + compact-direct] -> [one compact untrusted metadata line + body]
  [group/rich context] -> [existing full fenced metadata blocks]
```

## Security Impact (required)

  - New permissions/capabilities? (No)
  - Secrets/tokens handling changed? (No)
  - New/changed network calls? (No)
  - Command/tool execution surface changed? (No)
  - Data access scope changed? (No)
  - If any Yes, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

  - OS: macOS
  - Runtime/container: Node 22 / pnpm
  - Model/provider: N/A
  - Integration/channel (if any): Telegram/direct chat prompt assembly
  - Relevant config (redacted): messages.inboundMetadataMode: "compact-direct"

### Steps

  1. Configure messages.inboundMetadataMode: "compact-direct".
  2. Build inbound user context for a direct Telegram message.
  3. Compare prompt prefix against default/full mode and group-chat mode.

### Expected

  - Direct chats use one compact untrusted metadata line.
  - Sender name/username JSON block is not prepended in compact direct mode.
  - Groups and rich context retain full fenced metadata blocks.
  - Compact metadata does not appear in user-visible history.

### Actual

  - Matches expected after this PR.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

  What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

  - Verified scenarios:
      - pnpm check:changed
      - pnpm build
      - pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/inbound-meta.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/strip-inbound-meta.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run.media-only.test.ts
        src/plugins/cli.test.ts src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts
      - pnpm config:schema:check
      - pnpm config:docs:check
      - codex review --base origin/main
  - Edge cases checked: direct vs group chats, compact metadata stripping, lookalike user text preservation, default config schema generation, wiki/memory CLI
    scope review finding.
  - What you did not verify: Live Telegram bot delivery.

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

  - Backward compatible? (Yes)
  - Config/env changes? (Yes)
  - Migration needed? (No)
  - If yes, exact upgrade steps: Optional only. Set messages.inboundMetadataMode to "compact-direct" to enable the new direct-chat prompt format.

## Risks and Mitigations

List only real risks for this PR. Add/remove entries as needed. If none, write `None`.

  - Risk: Compact direct metadata could leak into user-visible history.
      - Mitigation: Added sanitizer support and tests for compact metadata stripping plus lookalike user text preservation.
  - Risk: Compacting metadata could remove context needed in groups or rich-message flows.
      - Mitigation: Compact mode applies only to direct chats; groups/replies/forwards/locations/history keep full metadata.
