target.title: fix(gateway): preserve abort after dispatch rejection
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- Preserves the terminal `chat.abort` outcome when `chat.send` dispatch rejects after the abort already completed.
- Preserves the real dispatch `error` outcome when abort arrives only during awaited error persistence, instead of letting the late abort mask or cache the error.
- Adds regressions for both race orderings and keeps stale abort tombstones from leaking into later same-id retries.

Why does this matter now?

- `chat.send` now caches terminal abort results for abort races. The post-dispatch rejection path still had two bad windows: abort-first could be overwritten by a late dispatch error, and error-first could expose `aborted` while the handler was still awaiting fallback persistence.
- The branch has been refreshed onto `main` after a merge conflict in the Gateway test file; the final pre-publish check against the latest `main` found no same-surface drift and a clean merge-tree.

What is the intended outcome?

- Same-run retry after an abort-first race observes the terminal timeout/aborted payload, not a synthetic dispatch error.
- Same-run retry after an error-first race observes the error while the error dedupe is live, and a later retry can start normally instead of replaying a stale abort marker.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- Client-side terminal ACK handling.
- Live provider/model E2E behavior.
- The original pre-dispatch attachment-preparation abort race, which is already fixed on `main`.

What does success look like?

- The PR diff stays limited to `src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts` and `src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts`.
- Focused Gateway regression, typecheck, lint, diff hygiene, production-handler proof, and real Gateway/WebSocket RPC proof all pass on the refreshed branch head.

What should reviewers focus on?

- The `.catch` path snapshots whether abort had already won before any awaited fallback persistence.
- The error-winning path closes abortability, clears late abort state, and writes same-run error dedupe before awaiting fallback persistence.
- The idempotent active-run cleanup prevents duplicate cleanup while removing the late abort/retry window.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Fixes #90999

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #84176 and #84306.

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed:

Post-dispatch `chat.send` rejection could produce contradictory terminal state around `chat.abort`: abort-first races could be overwritten by a dispatch error, and error-first races could briefly expose/cache `aborted` before the final dispatch `error`.

- Real environment tested:

Linux source checkout at local conflict-repair head `e16d6f5fcfeabe193b49883fe89061dead8efad1`, Node/Vitest through repo scripts. The merge commit has parents `74dbae230e9e5ecf80739ec7a0939130fe9ef670` and `7e12a3326dd2396ca39ceee079f4304966e92fb9`; exact main CI run `27505476408` for that base was green when proof was captured. Pre-publish fetch advanced `upstream/main` to `6134c00657a247d6f7accc9182d0b901c4f26b48` (3 commits, no Gateway/proof-surface overlap); merge-tree stayed clean and PR-style diff stayed two Gateway files. Current-base checks were not fully green at prep time, so post-push PR checks are the CI source of truth.

- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```shell
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test-pr91000-conflict2-e16.tsbuildinfo
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-pr91000-conflict2-e16.tsbuildinfo
node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts
git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD -- src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts
OPENCLAW_PROBE_ROOT=/tmp/openclaw-pr91000-conflict2-20260615T010132+0800.5Emd8e node --experimental-test-module-mocks --import tsx /tmp/pr91000-behavior-probe-dynamic.mts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/pr91000-websocket-proof-dynamic.test.ts
```

- Evidence after fix:

Terminal capture from this branch, copied live output:

```shell
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts
Test Files 2 passed (2)
Tests 82 passed (82)

$ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test-pr91000-conflict2-e16.tsbuildinfo
passed

$ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-pr91000-conflict2-e16.tsbuildinfo
passed

$ node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.

$ git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD -- src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts
no output

$ OPENCLAW_PROBE_ROOT=/tmp/openclaw-pr91000-conflict2-20260615T010132+0800.5Emd8e node --experimental-test-module-mocks --import tsx /tmp/pr91000-behavior-probe-dynamic.mts
source: production Gateway chatHandlers with controlled dispatch boundary and temporary config/session store
abort-first dispatch rejection: abortResponse.aborted=true, terminalDedupe.status=timeout, chatBroadcastStates=[aborted], errorBroadcastCount=0
error-first late abort: abortResponse.aborted=false, retryDuringPending.status=error, lateAbortTombstonePresentAfterError=false, midWindowChatBroadcastStates=[], chatBroadcastStates=[error], retry after deleting error dedupe starts

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/pr91000-websocket-proof-dynamic.test.ts
gateway-core: 2 tests passed
gateway-server: 2 tests passed
gateway-client: 2 tests passed
Test Files 3 passed (3)
Tests 6 passed (6)
```

Real Gateway + WebSocket RPC proof artifact from the last command:

```text
proofKind: real Gateway server + WebSocket RPC harness with controlled dispatch boundary
branchHead: e16d6f5fcfeabe193b49883fe89061dead8efad1
abort-first dispatch rejection over WebSocket:
  chat.send ACK status=started
  chat.abort response aborted=true, runIds=[ws-proof-abort-first]
  dispatch saw AbortSignal.aborted=true
  chat broadcast states=[aborted]
error-first late abort over WebSocket:
  chat.send ACK status=started
  late chat.abort response aborted=false, runIds=[]
  same-id chat.send retry during pending fallback returned cached status=error with UNAVAILABLE
  mid-window chat broadcast states=[]
  final chat broadcast states=[error]
```

- Observed result after fix:

Abort-first dispatch rejection preserves the terminal timeout/aborted result and emits only the aborted chat state. Error-first dispatch rejection makes the error terminal before awaited fallback persistence; late abort returns `aborted:false`, same-id retry during the pending fallback receives the cached error, no aborted chat state is emitted, and retry after deleting the error dedupe can start normally.

- What was not tested:

No browser UI, native client, or live provider/model E2E run was performed. The strongest transport proof uses a real Gateway server plus real WebSocket RPC client, while controlling only the dispatch boundary through the repo's normal Gateway test seam.

- Proof limitations or environment constraints:

The race is deterministic once dispatch rejection, fallback persistence, and abort timing are controlled. The production-handler probe and WebSocket proof both control that boundary so they can falsify the two terminal-state orderings without depending on a flaky live provider race.

- Before evidence (optional but encouraged):

```shell
Main at merge parent `7e12a3326dd2396ca39ceee079f4304966e92fb9` still reproduced both bad windows in the diagnostic production-handler probe before this branch's fix:
abort-first: chat.abort returned aborted=true, terminalDedupe.status=error, chatBroadcastStates=[aborted,error]
error-first: late chat.abort returned aborted=true, retryDuringPending.status=timeout, lateAbortTombstonePresentAfterError=true, chatBroadcastStates=[aborted,error]
```

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test-pr91000-conflict2-e16.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-pr91000-conflict2-e16.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts`
- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD -- src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_PROBE_ROOT=/tmp/openclaw-pr91000-conflict2-20260615T010132+0800.5Emd8e node --experimental-test-module-mocks --import tsx /tmp/pr91000-behavior-probe-dynamic.mts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/pr91000-websocket-proof-dynamic.test.ts`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Added/updated coverage for abort-before-dispatch-reject preserving terminal abort state.
- Added/updated coverage for dispatch-reject-before-late-abort preserving terminal error state, rejecting late abort, serving cached error to same-id retry while fallback persistence is pending, and allowing a later retry after error dedupe cleanup.
- Refreshed the branch onto `main` at `7e12a3326dd2396ca39ceee079f4304966e92fb9`; resolution keeps `removeTempDir(sessionDir)` cleanup and adjacent timing tests. Pre-publish fetch at `6134c00657a247d6f7accc9182d0b901c4f26b48` had no same-surface drift and clean merge-tree.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- The original abort-first regression failed because `chat:<runId>` cached `status:"error"` instead of the expected timeout/aborted payload.
- A later error-first probe found `chat.abort` could still return `aborted:true` during pending fallback persistence, and broadcasts could include both `aborted` and `error` for the same run.
- Current `main` still reproduced both bad windows before the refreshed branch was tested.

If no test was added, why not?

N/A.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Terminal abort/error ordering is now stable for post-dispatch rejection races.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

Abort/error ordering in the `chat.send` async cleanup path, especially while fallback transcript persistence is pending.

How is that risk mitigated?

Both orderings are covered: abort already visible at dispatch rejection, and dispatch error already terminal before a late abort arrives. The additional WebSocket proof exercises the real Gateway server and RPC transport shape while keeping the race timing deterministic.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Review the refreshed conflict-repair head and the updated real Gateway/WebSocket RPC proof.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

After the branch/body refresh, CI and maintainer/bot review are still pending.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

No new bot comment was addressed in this refresh. The refresh updates the exact base/head/proof evidence after conflict repair while preserving the existing proof scope.

target.title: fix(acp): strip chat options for Claude ACP sessions
target.body:
## Summary
- preserve startup ACP runtime options without replaying them as post-start controls
- strip chat-only model/thinking options from managed Claude ACP startup and resume paths
- keep timeout/cwd runtime options intact and add regressions for spawn, initialize, and restart/resume paths

## Tests
- node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/acp-spawn.test.ts src/acp/control-plane/manager.initialize-session.test.ts src/acp/control-plane/manager.runtime-handles.test.ts
- pnpm tsgo:core:all

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** Managed Claude ACP sessions should not fail because OpenClaw forwards chat/runtime controls such as model or thinking/effort as post-start Claude ACP config options. The patch strips those chat-only options for Claude ACP while preserving timeout/cwd handling.

- **Real environment tested:** Mac Mini M4 arm64 running the live OpenClaw gateway with managed Claude ACP enabled, plus the PR head source checkout at `cc542548a08a42eb950a393f7f7223c3af0aafe0` for focused regression tests.

- **Exact steps or command run after the patch:**
  1. Ran a live managed Claude ACP smoke through OpenClaw with deliberately bad chat-style options: `runtime=acp`, `agentId=claude`, `model=gpt-5.5`, `thinking=off`, task `Reply exactly ACP_SMOKE_OK and stop`.
  2. Read the resulting OpenClaw session history to confirm the Claude ACP session produced a normal assistant response instead of an ACP config-option failure.
  3. Ran the PR-head regression command: `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/acp-spawn.test.ts src/acp/control-plane/manager.initialize-session.test.ts src/acp/control-plane/manager.runtime-handles.test.ts`.

- **After-fix evidence:**
  Live OpenClaw/Claude ACP smoke output from the real gateway after the runtime fix:

  ```text
  openclaw sessions_spawn runtime=acp agentId=claude model=gpt-5.5 thinking=off task="Reply exactly ACP_SMOKE_OK and stop"
  status: accepted

  openclaw sessions_history <spawned Claude ACP session>
  user: Smoke test only. Reply exactly ACP_SMOKE_OK and stop. Do not inspect files or run commands.
  assistant: ACP_SMOKE_OK
  ```

  Redacted pre-fix live gateway failures from the same setup showed the rejected option this patch prevents:

  ```text
  2026-06-12T12:24Z AcpRuntimeError: Invalid value for config option effort: off: code=ACP_TURN_FAILED run=0b4a2d6a...
  2026-06-13T08:36Z AcpRuntimeError: Invalid value for config option effort: off: code=ACP_TURN_FAILED run=39f87d89...
  2026-06-13T09:46Z AcpRuntimeError: Invalid value for config option effort: off: code=ACP_TURN_FAILED run=01728a7d...
  2026-06-13T11:01Z AcpRuntimeError: Invalid value for config option effort: off: code=ACP_TURN_FAILED run=963d5346...
  ```

- **Observed result after fix:** The live managed Claude ACP smoke returned `ACP_SMOKE_OK`; it did not fail with the previous `Invalid value for config option effort: off` / `ACP_TURN_FAILED` error. On PR head `cc542548a08a42eb950a393f7f7223c3af0aafe0`, the focused regression command passed 86 tests across the three ACP/spawn test files, including the Claude-specific cases for stripping model/thinking before initialise and avoiding first-turn replay of startup model/thinking.

- **What was not tested:** I did not replace/restart the full installed gateway from this PR branch during the PR steward pass. The live smoke proves the same managed-Claude-ACP failure mode is fixed in the running OpenClaw setup; the PR-head test run proves this branch contains the matching ACP/spawn regression coverage.

target.title: fix(control-ui): validate Telegram cron To against full target contract (#90467)
target.body:
## Summary

Adds save-time Control UI validation for the cron **announce → To** field when
the delivery channel is Telegram, so a cron job cannot be saved with a recipient
Telegram can never deliver to. Previously the announce `To` field accepted any
string and the failure only surfaced at runtime (issue #90467).

### What changed since the first revision

The first revision validated **numeric chat ids only**. That was too narrow:
cron announce delivery resolves the target through
`normalizeTargetForProvider("telegram", to)` → the Telegram plugin's
`normalizeTelegramMessagingTarget` (`extensions/telegram/src/targets.ts`,
`parseTelegramTarget` + `normalizeTelegramLookupTarget`), which accepts more than
numeric ids. A numeric-only check would have blocked valid recipients such as
`@mychat`, `https://t.me/mychannel`, and `telegram:123` before save.

Validation now mirrors the full delivery contract:

- numeric chat ids (`-1001234567890`, `123456789`)
- `@username` and bare usernames (`[A-Za-z0-9_]{5,}`)
- `t.me` links (`t.me/...`, `https://t.me/...`)
- `telegram:` / `tg:` prefixes
- optional `:topicId` / `:topic:topicId` suffix on any of the above

Anything else (e.g. a phone number from the generic placeholder, or a hyphenated
name) is rejected before save. The Telegram plugin remains the canonical owner of
the contract; the UI regex is a documented save-time UX mirror, not a second
source of truth (the UI package cannot import plugin internals across the
`extensions/**` boundary).

Also in this PR:
- Telegram-specific placeholder/help copy on the announce `To` field.
- Inline error rendering + `aria-invalid` / `aria-describedby` on the announce
  `To` field (previously only the webhook field rendered its error).
- Regenerated i18n locale bundles + `.i18n` metadata for the new English strings
  (`pnpm ui:i18n:sync`), so the UI i18n drift gate stays green.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/cron.test.ts` → 43 passed
  (new cases: rejects phone/hyphenated/short-handle targets; accepts every
  Telegram form runtime resolves; ignores the format on non-Telegram channels).
- `node --import tsx scripts/control-ui-i18n.ts check` → all locales clean.
- `oxfmt --check` and `oxlint` clean on the changed source files.

### Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** cron announce `To` for Telegram now blocks unsendable
  targets at save time and accepts every form Telegram delivery resolves.
- **Real environment tested:** local Vitest (Control UI controller) + i18n gate.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/cron.test.ts`;
  `node --import tsx scripts/control-ui-i18n.ts check`.
- **Evidence after fix:** 43/43 controller tests pass; i18n check reports all
  locales clean.
- **Observed result after fix:** valid Telegram targets save; invalid ones show
  the inline `telegramChatIdInvalid` error.
- **What was not tested:** browser-rendered screenshot of the cron form
  helper/error states (logic is covered by controller unit tests; a maintainer
  can request Mantis visual proof if required before merge).

Closes #90467

- Red repro before fix: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/method-scopes.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/session-actions.contract.test.ts` failed with `plugin session action row exploded`.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/method-scopes.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/session-actions.contract.test.ts`

- `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/gateway/plugin-session-action-registrations.ts src/gateway/method-scopes.ts src/gateway/method-scopes.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/plugin-host-hooks.ts src/plugins/contracts/session-actions.contract.test.ts`

- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: malformed plugin session-action rows can no longer throw during dynamic operator-scope resolution or gateway session-action dispatch before a healthy sibling row is reached.

Real environment tested: local Codex worktree using the repo Vitest wrapper; broad Crabbox changed gate attempted but blocked before execution by coordinator auth.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/method-scopes.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/session-actions.contract.test.ts`

Evidence after fix: focused Vitest passed 2 shards covering gateway method scopes and plugin session-action contracts; local and branch autoreview both reported no accepted/actionable findings.

Observed result after fix: unreadable session-action rows are skipped, healthy action rows still resolve their declared scopes and dispatch, sparse `requiredScopes` no longer fails open, and sparse/empty scope lists keep the default write-scope requirement.

target.title: fix(discord): disable ws 8.21.0 receiver part limits for gateway sockets
target.body:
## Summary

ws 8.21.0 introduced `maxBufferedChunks` and `maxFragments` receiver limits (commit `[security] Limit retained message parts`) that can trigger `WS_ERR_TOO_MANY_BUFFERED_PARTS` and close connections with code 1008 under certain traffic patterns.

This PR explicitly sets both limits to `0` (disabled) for Discord gateway WebSocket connections, restoring the ws 8.20.0 behavior for the trusted Discord gateway peer while preserving the security fix for all other WebSocket usage.

## Changes

- **`extensions/discord/src/internal/gateway.ts`**: Base class `createWebSocket()` now passes `maxBufferedChunks: 0, maxFragments: 0` with a `ws.ClientOptions` cast (since `@types/ws` 8.18.1 does not yet expose these fields).
- **`extensions/discord/src/monitor/gateway-plugin.ts`**: Extended `DiscordGatewayWebSocketCtor` type to include the new options; monitor gateway creation path passes the same disabled limits.
- **`extensions/discord/src/internal/gateway.test.ts`**: Added test verifying base class WebSocket receives the expected options.
- **`extensions/discord/src/monitor/gateway-plugin.test.ts`**: Added test verifying monitor plugin WebSocket receives the expected options.
- **`extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.proxy.test.ts`**: Updated two existing exact-match assertions to include the new options.

## Why `0` instead of raising the limits

- ws 8.21.0 treats `0` as disabled (`receiver.js:61`, `receiver.js:98`: `if (this._maxBufferedChunks > 0 && ...)`), matching the pre-8.21.0 behavior exactly.
- Discord gateway is a trusted peer; the security limits are designed to protect against untrusted senders.
- Choosing an arbitrary higher threshold would be fragile without real production traffic data to calibrate against.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: ws 8.21.0's retained-part receiver limits (`maxFragments` default 131072 / `maxBufferedChunks` default 1048576) abort a Discord gateway socket with `WS_ERR_TOO_MANY_BUFFERED_PARTS` / close `1008`; this PR sets both to `0` (disabled) on the Discord gateway sockets, restoring ws 8.20.0 behavior for that trusted peer.
- **Real environment tested**: Live Discord gateway connection from a Docker `node:22-bookworm-slim` container (Node `v22.22.3`) with the repo-pinned `ws@8.21.0` installed exactly, using a real bot token (redacted), connecting to `wss://gateway.discord.gg/?v=10&encoding=json`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: build a `node:22-bookworm-slim` image with `npm install ws@8.21.0`, then run a client that constructs the gateway socket with this patch's options (`new ws.WebSocket(url, { handshakeTimeout: 30000, maxBufferedChunks: 0, maxFragments: 0 })`), completes the HELLO/IDENTIFY/READY handshake against the real Discord gateway, sends heartbeats, holds a 60s stable window, then closes with code 1000.
- **Evidence after fix**: Live Discord gateway run with this patch's options applied (token, `session_id`, and `resume_gateway_url` redacted):

```
[env] node=v22.22.3 ws=8.21.0
[discord] connecting wss://gateway.discord.gg (maxBufferedChunks:0, maxFragments:0)
[discord] open
[discord] HELLO heartbeat_interval=41250ms
[discord] IDENTIFY sent (token redacted)
[discord] READY received (session_id/resume_gateway_url redacted)
[discord] HEARTBEAT_ACK #1
[discord] stable window 60000ms complete
[discord] close code=1000 reason=<empty>
[result] PASS live: READY + 1 heartbeat ACK; no WS_ERR_TOO_MANY_BUFFERED_PARTS during stable window
```

Supplemental deterministic mechanism repro against a local ws 8.21.0 gateway streaming one message in 131073 fragments (one over the client default `maxFragments`), exercising the same `ws.WebSocket` receiver path:

```
# default ws.WebSocket (ws 8.21 defaults)
[before] open
[before] error code=WS_ERR_TOO_MANY_BUFFERED_PARTS message=Too many message fragments
[before] close code=1006 reason=<empty>

# this patch's options applied
[after] open
[after] message received: 131073 bytes - NO receiver-limit close
```

- **Observed result after fix**: On the real Discord gateway, with the disabled limits applied, the connection reaches `READY`, exchanges heartbeats, and stays connected through the 60s window with no `WS_ERR_TOO_MANY_BUFFERED_PARTS` and no `1008` close; the only close is the intentional client-initiated `1000`. The deterministic repro confirms the same constructor options remove the receiver-limit close that the default ws 8.21 client hits.
- **What was not tested**: the live run confirms a real gateway session stays healthy with the patched options, but does not force Discord's production fragmentation pattern on demand, does not exercise the `zlib-stream` transport, and does not drive the full OpenClaw plugin wiring end-to-end. The deterministic repro proves the ws 8.21 retained-part mechanism and that this patch's options disable the exact receiver checks, but uses a local peer rather than Discord's live framing.

## Tests

- `gateway.test.ts`: 25 passed
- `gateway-plugin.test.ts`: 17 passed  
- `provider.proxy.test.ts`: 21 passed
- TypeScript check (tsgo): clean

Fixes #88330

target.title: fix(text): normalize CJK/fullwidth quotes in reasoning tag delimiters
target.body:
## Summary

Models that emit inline thinking content at stream boundaries may insert CJK fullwidth quotation marks (U+300C-U+300F: 「」❲❳) in place of ASCII angle brackets around reasoning tag names. The pattern matcher uses ASCII brackets, so fullwidth-delimited tags would silently pass through `stripReasoningTagsFromText` without being stripped, causing raw reasoning text to leak into chat bubbles.

This change normalizes fullwidth brackets to their ASCII equivalents before applying the `QUICK_TAG_RE` early-return check and the main tag-stripping loop, so that both ASCII and fullwidth variants are handled identically. The original input string is returned unchanged when no tag markers are present.

## Changes

- `src/shared/text/reasoning-tags.ts`: Normalize U+300C-U+300F to `<>` before pattern matching
- `src/shared/text/reasoning-tags.test.ts`: Add test cases for fullwidth quote variants

## Verification

- TypeScript compilation: `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit` passes
- oxlint: `pnpm exec oxlint --quiet src/shared/text/reasoning-tags.ts src/shared/text/reasoning-tags.test.ts` passes

Closes #88702

target.title: fix(windows): disable scheduled task before stop to prevent PT3M re-trigger during update
target.body:
fix(windows): disable scheduled task before stop to prevent PT3M re-trigger during update

The Windows scheduled task uses a PT3M LogonTrigger which repeats every 3 minutes.
Previously, stopScheduledTask only called schtasks /End, leaving the trigger active.
During an openclaw update (which can take several minutes), the trigger would
re-activate the gateway mid-update, causing a race condition.

This change adds /DISABLE after /End in stopScheduledTask, and /ENABLE before
re-launch in restartScheduledTask (since runScheduledTaskOrThrow re-creates the
task afresh and would otherwise start it enabled).

target.title: fix(coding-agent): strip Claude Code nesting env vars when spawning claude (#57858)
target.body:
## Summary

- Coding-agent skill SKILL.md now spawns Claude Code with `env -u CLAUDECODE -u CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID -u CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT claude ...` so a nested OpenClaw-inside-Claude-Code dispatch does not inherit the parent's nesting guard and silently no-op.
- Managed Claude CLI backend (`extensions/anthropic/cli-shared.ts` `CLAUDE_CLI_CLEAR_ENV`) now strips `CLAUDECODE` and `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID`; `CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT` was already on the list but was the only nesting var covered.
- **Correction from the prior revision (ClawSweeper P1):** the earlier fix added `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION` (no `_ID`), but a live Claude Code process does not export that variable. The real nesting marker current Claude Code sets is `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID`. Verified env keys on a live Claude Code child: `CLAUDECODE`, `CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT`, `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID`, `CLAUDE_CODE_EXECPATH`, `CLAUDE_CODE_TMPDIR` — there is no `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION`. So the prior revision stripped a non-existent var while the real marker `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID` still leaked into the spawned child, leaving the silent-nested-exit bug unfixed for current Claude Code. This revision adds `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID` to the production list, the skill recipe, and both list-parity tests. The shorter `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION` is kept in the production `CLAUDE_CLI_CLEAR_ENV` only as a documented harmless no-op (deleting an absent key is a no-op) to also cover any older/future shorter-named variant; the shell recipe uses the real `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID`.
- Why now: reporter saw 0/6 dispatched workers produce any output (no commits, no errors) when OpenClaw itself was running inside a Claude Code session. Claude Code's own nesting guard reads `CLAUDECODE=1`, sees a parent session, and exits without writing to stdout/stderr.
- Intended outcome: dispatched Claude Code workers run the task instead of self-exiting, both when spawned via the bash tool inside the coding-agent skill (`env -u ...` in the recipe) and via the managed CLI backend (`clearEnv` strips the inherited markers).
- Out of scope: the global host-exec env-strip list in `src/infra/host-env-security-policy.json` is intentionally not touched. That policy applies to every host child process and stripping these vars there would change behavior for arbitrary bash tool callers, well beyond Claude Code workers. The skill recipe + Claude CLI backend strip together cover both surfaces this issue describes without expanding scope.
- Success: a dispatched Claude Code worker started from inside an OpenClaw-inside-Claude-Code session produces output and completes the requested task; existing Claude CLI tests still pass.
- Reviewer focus: skill recipe wording (product-visible), the comment block in `cli-shared.ts` explaining why the vars are stripped and why `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION` is kept as a no-op, and that the two test files were kept in sync with the production list.

## Linked context

Closes #57858

Related: none beyond the issue.

Not requested by a maintainer — picked up via ClawSweeper triage.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Claude Code's nesting guard silently exits when OpenClaw dispatches it as a child while OpenClaw itself runs inside a Claude Code session, because the parent's nesting markers (`CLAUDECODE`, `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID`, `CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT`) leak into the child. Reporter saw 6/6 background coding-agent workers produce zero output, zero errors, zero commits.
- Real environment tested: local macOS arm64 source checkout (worktree), Node 22. Targeted Vitest runs for the touched units plus a real spawned-child env capture using the production `CLAUDE_CLI_CLEAR_ENV` list.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/anthropic/cli-shared.test.ts src/agents/cli-backends.test.ts`
  - `npx oxfmt --check extensions/anthropic/cli-shared.ts extensions/anthropic/cli-shared.test.ts src/agents/cli-backends.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs extensions/anthropic/cli-shared.ts extensions/anthropic/cli-shared.test.ts src/agents/cli-backends.test.ts`
  - `npx markdownlint-cli2 --config config/markdownlint-cli2.jsonc skills/coding-agent/SKILL.md`
  - real-child proof: `node --import tsx` script that imports the production `CLAUDE_CLI_CLEAR_ENV`, sets `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID` in the parent, applies the production strip loop, and spawns a real `child_process` to read its inherited env back (verbatim output below).
- Evidence after fix:
  - `cli-shared.test.ts`: 18 passed (now asserts `CLAUDECODE`, `CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT`, `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID`, and `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION` are all in the strip list).
  - `cli-backends.test.ts`: 46 passed (fixture `clearEnv` updated to keep the mocked runtime entry in parity with the production list, including `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID`).
  - oxfmt / oxlint / markdownlint: 0 errors.
- Observed result after fix: managed CLI backend env strip now covers the real nesting marker `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID`; the child-env capture below spawns a real subprocess through the production strip path and confirms `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID` (and `CLAUDECODE`, `CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT`) are gone from the child while an unrelated var survives. The coding-agent skill recipe now shows the `env -u CLAUDECODE -u CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID -u CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT` prefix in both the hard-rules block and the worked Claude Code launch example.
- What was not tested: no live Claude Code binary was launched from inside a nested OpenClaw-in-Claude-Code session (no Claude Code install in this environment). The reporter's environment is the canonical repro; the mechanism that fed the bug (inherited nesting vars in the child env) is demonstrably neutralized for the real marker name.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: skill-level changes are documentation/recipe edits that take effect when a session next loads the skill; there is no runtime unit test surface for the recipe text itself, only the CLI backend code path.
- Before evidence (optional): n/a; the bug is a silent no-op, the symptom is empty worker output reported in #57858.

### Sibling-surface check

- Codex CLI (`extensions/codex/...`): Codex does not have an analogous self-nesting guard environment variable; no equivalent silent-exit happens when its env is inherited from a parent OpenClaw session. The Codex backend already has its own `clearEnv` list for auth/config keys (`auth-bridge.ts`, `config.ts`) and is not affected by this fix.
- OpenCode CLI: same as Codex — no documented nesting guard variable; no behavior change needed.
- Crabbox sandbox subprocess: runs in an isolated container and does not inherit the host `CLAUDECODE` env into a nested Claude run, so the silent-exit symptom does not surface there.
- Subagent spawn paths (`src/agents/cli-runner/execute.ts`): consume the backend `clearEnv` list, so they automatically pick up the new entry once `CLAUDE_CLI_CLEAR_ENV` is updated. No separate change required.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/anthropic/cli-shared.test.ts src/agents/cli-backends.test.ts` (3 files, 64 tests passed)
- `npx oxfmt --check` on touched files (0 errors)
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs` on touched files (0 errors)
- `npx markdownlint-cli2 --config config/markdownlint-cli2.jsonc skills/coding-agent/SKILL.md` (0 errors)

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- `extensions/anthropic/cli-shared.test.ts`: new assert that `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID` is in `CLAUDE_CLI_CLEAR_ENV` (alongside the existing `CLAUDECODE`, `CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT`, `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION` asserts) to lock in the real nesting-marker strip.
- `src/agents/cli-backends.test.ts`: fixture `clearEnv` kept in parity with the production list (now includes `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID`) so the simulated runtime backend entry reflects the same shape.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- No automated test failed. The visible failure was reporter's observation: 0/6 background dispatched Claude Code workers produced any output, because the inherited nesting guard caused Claude Code to exit silently. The prior revision of this PR appeared to address it but stripped the wrong var name (`CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION` instead of `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID`).

If no test was added, why not?

- The skill-level recipe edit is documentation surface (markdown text in `skills/coding-agent/SKILL.md`); per AGENTS.md skill doc text changes do not require a new Vitest. The CLI backend change does have updated test asserts; see above.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`) Yes — the coding-agent skill now ships a slightly different Claude Code launch command (`env -u CLAUDECODE -u CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID -u CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT ...`). Users dispatching workers via this skill on a fresh session will see the corrected `env -u ...` prefix on the bash command.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`) Yes (environment only) — the managed Claude CLI child env now also strips `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID` (the real marker) plus `CLAUDECODE`; `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION` is retained as a harmless no-op. No config schema, defaults, or migrations changed.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No. The added strip targets are Claude Code's own nesting markers; they are not auth tokens, network endpoints, or tool-execution gates. No new permissions, no new network reachability.

What is the highest-risk area? Skill recipe wording — it is product-visible documentation copied into worker prompts. If the `env -u ...` syntax is mistyped users would hit a shell error instead of the silent exit they had before, which is observable and easy to diagnose.

How is that risk mitigated? Used the standard POSIX `env -u VAR` form (supported by macOS and Linux `env`); kept the rest of the command identical; the hard-rule bullet names the issue number and the real marker name so anyone editing the recipe later sees the rationale.

## Current review state

What is the next action? Maintainer / ClawSweeper re-review of the corrected `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID` marker across the production list, the skill recipe, and the parity tests.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof? The end-to-end env-stripping mechanism is proven with a real spawned child against the production list — see "Real behavior proof — child-env capture" below. The only thing still not exercised is an actual Claude Code *binary* started under a nested session (no Claude Code install here); the mechanism that fed the bug (inherited nesting vars in the child env) is now demonstrably neutralized for the real marker `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID`.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed? ClawSweeper P1: "Add `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID` to the production clearEnv list, coding-agent recipe, and both tests wherever `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION` was added; refresh the proof to show `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID` is stripped from the child." Done in this revision.

## Real behavior proof — child-env capture

ClawSweeper's P1 asked to correct the var name to `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID` and refresh the proof to show that real marker is stripped from the spawned child. Below is a reproducible, executable proof against the **real** production `CLAUDE_CLI_CLEAR_ENV` list — no Claude Code binary required, because the bug is purely that the nesting guard vars leak into the spawned child's environment. We prove the real marker no longer leaks.

**How real it is (production seams):**

- `CLAUDE_CLI_CLEAR_ENV` is imported verbatim from `extensions/anthropic/cli-shared.ts` — the exact list this PR edits. The Claude backend wires it straight in as `clearEnv: [...CLAUDE_CLI_CLEAR_ENV]`.
- The production runner derives the child env from `process.env` and then deletes each `clearEnv` key (`src/agents/cli-runner/execute.ts:400`: `delete next[key]`). The proof mirrors exactly that delete loop, fed the real imported constant.
- The marker name `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID` matches the live Claude Code env empirically: a live Claude Code process exposes `CLAUDECODE`, `CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT`, `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID`, `CLAUDE_CODE_EXECPATH`, `CLAUDE_CODE_TMPDIR` — there is no `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION`. The proof's PART C uses `CLAUDE_CODE_EXECPATH` (a real Claude Code var that is intentionally **not** in the strip list) as a survivor control, confirming the strip is targeted rather than blanket.

**What it does and asserts:**

- **A** sets `CLAUDECODE=1`, `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID=fake-session-id-abc123`, `CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT=cli`, plus `CLAUDE_CODE_EXECPATH` and a control var `OPENCLAW_PROOF_KEEPME=keep` in the parent (simulating OpenClaw running under Claude Code).
- **B** imports the production list and applies the delete loop; asserts the list contains `CLAUDECODE`, `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID` (real marker), `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION` (no-op), and `CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT`.
- **C** spawns a **real** child (`node -e ...` via `child_process.spawnSync`) with the production-built env and reads its env back: `CLAUDECODE`, `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID`, `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION`, and `CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT` are all `null`; the non-listed `CLAUDE_CODE_EXECPATH` and the unrelated `OPENCLAW_PROOF_KEEPME` survive.
- **D** negative control: spawns the same child with the raw parent env (no strip) and shows `CLAUDECODE=1` and `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID=fake-session-id-abc123` ARE inherited — proving the parent really carried them and the strip is what removes them (the bug reproduced).
- **E** asserts the `SKILL.md` `env -u CLAUDECODE -u CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID -u CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT` recipe is present and that the misnamed `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION` no longer appears in the recipe.

**Captured output** (verbatim from a local `node --import tsx` run; user-specific paths redacted):

```text
=== PART A: parent env (simulate OpenClaw running under Claude Code) ===
  parent CLAUDECODE               = 1
  parent CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID   = fake-session-id-abc123
  parent CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT   = cli
  parent CLAUDE_CODE_EXECPATH     = /usr/local/bin/claude
  parent OPENCLAW_PROOF_KEEPME    = keep
  parent PATH present             = true

=== PART B: build child env through the PRODUCTION clearEnv path ===
  imported CLAUDE_CLI_CLEAR_ENV (from extensions/anthropic/cli-shared.ts):
  ["ANTHROPIC_API_KEY","ANTHROPIC_API_KEY_OLD","ANTHROPIC_API_TOKEN","ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN","ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL","ANTHROPIC_CUSTOM_HEADERS","ANTHROPIC_OAUTH_TOKEN","ANTHROPIC_UNIX_SOCKET","CLAUDECODE","CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR","CLAUDE_CODE_API_KEY_FILE_DESCRIPTOR","CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT","CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_REFRESH_TOKEN","CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_SCOPES","CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN","CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN_FILE_DESCRIPTOR","CLAUDE_CODE_PLUGIN_CACHE_DIR","CLAUDE_CODE_PLUGIN_SEED_DIR","CLAUDE_CODE_REMOTE","CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION","CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID","CLAUDE_CODE_USE_COWORK_PLUGINS","CLAUDE_CODE_USE_BEDROCK","CLAUDE_CODE_USE_FOUNDRY","CLAUDE_CODE_USE_VERTEX","OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT","OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_HEADERS","OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_LOGS_ENDPOINT","OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_LOGS_HEADERS","OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_LOGS_PROTOCOL","OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_METRICS_ENDPOINT","OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_METRICS_HEADERS","OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_METRICS_PROTOCOL","OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_PROTOCOL","OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TRACES_ENDPOINT","OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TRACES_HEADERS","OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TRACES_PROTOCOL","OTEL_LOGS_EXPORTER","OTEL_METRICS_EXPORTER","OTEL_SDK_DISABLED","OTEL_TRACES_EXPORTER"]
  ok: CLAUDE_CLI_CLEAR_ENV contains CLAUDECODE
  ok: CLAUDE_CLI_CLEAR_ENV contains CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID (real marker, this PR)
  ok: CLAUDE_CLI_CLEAR_ENV still contains CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION (harmless no-op)
  ok: CLAUDE_CLI_CLEAR_ENV contains CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT

=== PART C: spawn a REAL child with the production-built (stripped) env ===
  child stdout (stripped env): {"CLAUDECODE":null,"CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID":null,"CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION":null,"CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT":null,"CLAUDE_CODE_EXECPATH":"/usr/local/bin/claude","OPENCLAW_PROOF_KEEPME":"keep"}
  ok: child does NOT see CLAUDECODE
  ok: child does NOT see CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID (real marker stripped)
  ok: child does NOT see CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION
  ok: child does NOT see CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT
  ok: child STILL sees unrelated OPENCLAW_PROOF_KEEPME=keep (strip is targeted)

=== PART D: negative control — spawn child WITHOUT stripping ===
  child stdout (raw parent env): {"CLAUDECODE":"1","CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID":"fake-session-id-abc123","CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION":null,"CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT":"cli","CLAUDE_CODE_EXECPATH":"/usr/local/bin/claude","OPENCLAW_PROOF_KEEPME":"keep"}
  ok: without strip, child INHERITS CLAUDECODE=1 (reproduces the bug)
  ok: without strip, child INHERITS CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID (reproduces the bug)

=== PART E: assert the SKILL.md `env -u` recipe fix ===
  ok: SKILL.md recipe strips CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID via env -u
  ok: SKILL.md no longer references the misnamed CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION in the recipe

ALL ASSERTIONS PASSED
exit=0
```

Narrative: the parent carried the real nesting marker `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID`; the production `CLAUDE_CLI_CLEAR_ENV` strip removed it (and `CLAUDECODE` / `CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT`) from the spawned child while preserving the non-listed `CLAUDE_CODE_EXECPATH` and the unrelated `OPENCLAW_PROOF_KEEPME` (strip is targeted, not blanket). The negative control confirms the parent actually carried `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID` (so absence in the stripped child is caused by the strip, not by the var never being set), and the `SKILL.md` `env -u` recipe is asserted to use the corrected marker. `ALL ASSERTIONS PASSED`, exit 0.

Note on `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION` vs `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID`: `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION` was a misnamed target in the prior revision and is corrected to `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID` (the marker current Claude Code actually exports). `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION` is retained in the production `CLAUDE_CLI_CLEAR_ENV` list only as a harmless belt-and-suspenders no-op (deleting an absent key is a no-op) in case an older/future Claude Code uses the shorter name; the shell recipe uses the real `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID`.

## Real behavior proof (gate-field summary)

- Behavior addressed: Claude Code nesting markers leaking from a parent OpenClaw-in-Claude-Code session into managed Claude CLI child processes, causing child Claude Code workers to exit silently with no output.
- Real environment tested: local macOS arm64 OpenClaw source worktree, Node 22, real `child_process` spawn using the production `CLAUDE_CLI_CLEAR_ENV` list imported from `extensions/anthropic/cli-shared.ts`; no mocks of the env-strip list.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran the documented `node --import tsx` proof script described above, plus targeted Vitest coverage for `extensions/anthropic/cli-shared.test.ts` and `src/agents/cli-backends.test.ts`.
- Evidence after fix: terminal output above shows `child stdout (stripped env): {"CLAUDECODE":null,"CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID":null,"CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION":null,"CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT":null,"CLAUDE_CODE_EXECPATH":"/usr/local/bin/claude","OPENCLAW_PROOF_KEEPME":"keep"}` followed by `ALL ASSERTIONS PASSED`, exit 0.
- Observed result after fix: the spawned child no longer receives `CLAUDECODE`, `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID`, or `CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT`; the negative-control child without stripping inherits those markers, and the unrelated survivor variable remains present, proving the strip is targeted.
- What was not tested: no live Claude Code binary was launched from inside a nested OpenClaw-in-Claude-Code session in this environment; the proof exercises the production environment-stripping mechanism that feeds that child process.

Without this PR, zh-CN users see raw English text in those UI areas and inconsistent/wrong translations in existing areas. - **Intended outcome**: zh-CN users get a fully translated, terminology-consistent, and naturally readable Control UI. All new activity strings translated, ~80 existing strings fixed or polished. - **Out of scope**: Other locale files (only zh-CN touched). Structural changes to the i18n system. Adding/modifying English source strings. RTL locale completeness (only format fixed for ar/fa). - **Success**: All 27 activity keys show Chinese text. Terminology is cross-section consistent (发送 for delivery, 偏移/分散 for jitter/stagger, 梦境 for dreaming). No English residuals in translated strings. passes clean.`pnpm run control-ui-i18n` ## Linked context - Related: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/85778 had translations that were later overwritten by CI locale refresh during merge. ## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs) - **Behavior addressed**: zh-CN UI translation accuracy and completeness. - **Real environment tested**: Local fork of openclaw at commit , built and deployed on a self-hosted Ubuntu 22.04 VM (OpenClaw Gateway v2026.5.20), viewed in Edge browser. - **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** ``` cd ui pnpm build # vite build → ✓ 1.40s pnpm run control-ui-i18n # locale check cp -r dist/control-ui/ /control-ui/ openclaw gateway --port 18789 # start → serves updated UI ``` - **Evidence after fix**: ``` zh-CN: clean (fallbacks=0) ``` The 27 fallbacks are pre-existing keys awaiting upstream AI provider validation (no local AI provider configured). All new activity keys are translated and the check detects no issues. - **Observed result**: All changed/dirty keys pass the sync check. No regression in other pending locales. File changed: 1 file, +105/−106 lines. Gateway serves Control UI at with zh-CN rendering correctly.`local18789` - **What was not tested**: Runtime UI rendering across all 18 supported language pairs (only zh-CN verified). Mobile/tablet viewport rendering. - **Proof limitations**: Tested on headless VM — no screenshot capture tool available. Fallback count of 27 is a pre-existing limitation of running without an AI provider on this setup — upstream CI will clear those on merge. - **Before evidence**: The original file had strings like (wrong — light color mode, not sleep), (inconsistent with later usage), (English-only in some keys).`common.light: "浅睡"``cron.runs.delivery: "投递"``dreaming.*: "Dreaming…"` ## Tests and validation - Commands run: ``` pnpm run control-ui-i18n # clean pass cd ui && pnpm build # vite build → 1.40s, no errors ``` - **Regression coverage**: Every modified key was verified pairwise against and checked with the sync script to ensure no sync-side conflicts.`en.ts` - **If no test added, why**: This is a locale-only change — there is no behavior logic to unit test. The i18n check script is the appropriate validation. ## Risk checklist - **User-visible behavior change?** — zh-CN locale text changes. No behavioral or logic changes.`Yes` - **Config, environment, or migration behavior change?** `No` - **Security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change?** `No` - **Highest-risk area**: None. Only string values changed; if any translation is inaccurate, the worst case is a confusing label — no security or data integrity impact. - **Risk mitigation**: Every changed key was verified against the English source for semantic accuracy. Consistent glossary terms were applied across sections. The locale check script confirmed no structural issues.

target.title: fix(status): detect shell-wrapped gateway services
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes #81751.
- Teaches gateway service audit to inspect shell-wrapped LaunchAgent command strings such as `/bin/zsh -lc "... index.js gateway --port 18890"`.
- Reuses the expanded command tokens for gateway port detection so shell-wrapped services do not miss `--port`.
- Adds coverage for the macOS LaunchAgent wrapper shape and a negative `--gateway-url` near-match case.

## Verification

- `/opt/homebrew/opt/node/bin/node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/daemon/service-audit.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/daemon/service-audit.ts src/daemon/service-audit.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: `openclaw gateway status --deep` could report `Service command does not include the gateway subcommand` for a real macOS LaunchAgent whose `ProgramArguments` are `/bin/zsh -lc "... openclaw gateway --port 18890"`.
- Real environment tested: macOS user LaunchAgent at `~/Library/LaunchAgents/ai.openclaw.enterprise.plist` from my local OpenClaw enterprise profile, evaluated with this branch's `src/daemon/service-audit.ts` implementation.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran a Node/tsx console probe that read the real LaunchAgent plist with `readLaunchAgentProgramArgumentsFromFile`, then passed that real command into `auditGatewayServiceConfig({ expectedPort: 18890, platform: "darwin" })`.
- Evidence after fix: terminal output from the real LaunchAgent probe:

```text
plist: ~/Library/LaunchAgents/ai.openclaw.enterprise.plist
programArguments.length: 3
gatewayPort: 18890
has gateway-command-missing: false
has gateway-port-mismatch: false
audit issue codes: gateway-path-missing-dirs, gateway-path-nonminimal
```

- Observed result after fix: the shell-wrapped LaunchAgent command now yields `gatewayPort: 18890`; the audit no longer emits `gateway-command-missing` or `gateway-port-mismatch` for this real service command. The remaining PATH findings are unrelated to gateway subcommand/port parsing.
- What was not tested: full packaged CLI replacement was not installed over the local npm package; this proof exercises the patched audit implementation against the real LaunchAgent plist. The targeted test and formatter checks above cover the source change.

## Notes

- I could not use `pnpm` directly in this shell because `pnpm` is not on PATH and `corepack pnpm` fails under the local Node 26/Corepack combination with `ERR_VM_DYNAMIC_IMPORT_CALLBACK_MISSING`. The commands above use the repo test wrapper directly with the Homebrew Node binary and the checked-in dependency bin.

target.title: fix(telegram): honor outbound reaction directives
target.body:
## Summary

- Parse Telegram reaction directives without leaking them into visible chat text.
- Preserve reaction intent in structured `channelData.telegram.reaction`.
- Support reaction-only replies when a valid target exists.
- Use Telegram's native reaction API instead of simulating the behavior in text.
- Reject missing targets and unsuccessful reaction delivery.

## Linked context

Closes #71140

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: Telegram reaction directives now normalize into native reaction payloads instead of leaking as visible text or being dropped.
- Real environment tested: OpenClaw Telegram SUT on macOS source checkout, with focused Vitest coverage for the production reply normalization and Telegram delivery paths.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/reply-delivery.test.ts src/infra/outbound/payloads.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/outbound-adapter.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot/delivery.test.ts
```

- Evidence after fix:

```text
Test Files  4 passed (4)
Tests       134 passed (134)
[test] passed 3 Vitest shards
```

- Observed result after fix: Reaction directives are stripped from visible text, reaction-only payloads reach the Telegram reaction API, and rejected or targetless reactions are not reported as successful delivery.
- What was not tested: A live Telegram account was not exercised from this source checkout. The linked issue contains live Telegram reproduction and prior installed-build validation.
- Negative control / before evidence: Current `main` does not parse reaction directives into structured channel data and rejects reaction-only replies as missing text/media.

## Tests and validation

- Targeted Vitest: 134 tests passed
- Focused core and Telegram lint passed
- Formatting check passed
- `git diff --check` passed
- Core-to-plugin boundary check passed

## Risk checklist

- Did user-visible behavior change? Yes
- Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? No
- Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? No
- Highest-risk area: Telegram reaction-only delivery
- Mitigation: Focused parser, normalization, adapter, invalid-reaction, and delivery regression tests

## Current review state

- AI-assisted: Yes, Codex
- Next action: Awaiting maintainer review
- Bot comments addressed: None yet

target.title: fix(compaction): make overflow tuning configurable
target.body:
## Summary

- add `agents.defaults.compaction.preemptiveOverflowRatio` for Pi tool-loop preemptive overflow threshold tuning
- add `agents.defaults.compaction.maxOverflowAttempts` for Pi overflow compaction retry tuning
- preserve current defaults (`0.9` ratio, `3` overflow retries) and document schema labels/help text

Fixes #80638
Fixes #80639

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Pi compaction overflow tuning is now configurable instead of hardcoded: configured values are accepted by the config schema and used by runtime resolver helpers, while unset config preserves the old defaults.
- **Real environment tested:** Harry's Mac mini, Darwin 25.4.0 arm64, Node v22.22.0, local OpenClaw checkout on branch `fix/80638-80639-compaction-knobs`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
node --import tsx - <<'EOF'
import { resolveMaxOverflowCompactionAttempts, resolvePreemptiveOverflowRatio } from './src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compaction-runtime-config.ts';
import { AgentDefaultsSchema } from './src/config/zod-schema.agent-defaults.ts';

const config = { agents: { defaults: { compaction: { preemptiveOverflowRatio: 0.72, maxOverflowAttempts: 1 } } } };
const parsed = AgentDefaultsSchema.parse(config.agents.defaults);
console.log('runtime preemptiveOverflowRatio:', resolvePreemptiveOverflowRatio(config));
console.log('runtime maxOverflowAttempts:', resolveMaxOverflowCompactionAttempts(config));
console.log('schema preserved preemptiveOverflowRatio:', parsed.compaction?.preemptiveOverflowRatio);
console.log('schema preserved maxOverflowAttempts:', parsed.compaction?.maxOverflowAttempts);
console.log('default preemptiveOverflowRatio:', resolvePreemptiveOverflowRatio({}));
console.log('default maxOverflowAttempts:', resolveMaxOverflowCompactionAttempts({}));
EOF
```

- **Evidence after fix:** terminal output from the real local checkout:

```text
runtime preemptiveOverflowRatio: 0.72
runtime maxOverflowAttempts: 1
schema preserved preemptiveOverflowRatio: 0.72
schema preserved maxOverflowAttempts: 1
default preemptiveOverflowRatio: 0.9
default maxOverflowAttempts: 3
```

- **Observed result after fix:** Configured values (`0.72`, `1`) are parsed and resolved at runtime; default behavior remains `0.9` and `3` when unset.
- **What was not tested:** A live long-running overflow failure against a paid provider; validation used the real config/runtime code paths directly.

## Validation

- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compaction-runtime-config.test.ts src/config/config.compaction-settings.test.ts src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts`
- `pnpm -s config:schema:check`
- `pnpm -s check:test-types`

target.title: SUP-1563 restore channel responsiveness health
target.body:
## Summary
- Surface real busy/active-run/stale activity state in channel status instead of relying only on connected transport state.
- Record Telegram outbound activity with the correct account id and avoid treating bot-authored Discord messages as human inbound activity.
- Return visible Discord/Telegram acknowledgements when a message is queued or steered behind active channel work, so connected transports do not look silent during busy turns.

## Real behavior proof
- Behavior or issue addressed: Discord/Telegram transports could be connected while Jarvis appeared silent because a human Discord message was accepted behind active channel work. The fix exposes busy/stale channel work state, prevents false Discord inbound timestamps from bot-authored gateway events, and keeps Telegram accounts connected while Discord proof is checked against the latest human message.
- Real environment tested: Andrew's Mac mini OpenClaw install, gateway on `127.0.0.1:18789`, installed OpenClaw 2026.5.3 runtime plus the SUP-1563 live hotfix, Discord bot account `default`, Telegram accounts `chad`, `default`, `michael`, and `steve`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node scripts/openclaw-discord-proof-check.js --json --allow-missing`
  - `curl -fsS --max-time 8 http://127.0.0.1:18789/readyz`
  - `sqlite3 -header -column /Users/andrew/.openclaw/tasks/runs.sqlite "select task_id, source_id, owner_key, runtime, status, datetime(created_at/1000,'unixepoch','localtime') as created, datetime(last_event_at/1000,'unixepoch','localtime') as last_event, substr(error,1,120) as error from task_runs where status in ('running','queued') order by created_at desc limit 20;"`
- Evidence after fix: live terminal output from the verifier showed `completionReady: true`, `gatewayReady: true`, `discordConnected: true`, `discordTurnNotStale: true`, `telegramAllConnected: true`, and `latestDiscordHumanPingHasLaterJarvisReplyOrAck: true`. The latest human Discord message `1503542519265361981` at `2026-05-11T23:41:11.062Z` had later Jarvis reply `1503543303164006632` at `2026-05-11T23:44:17.958Z`. Channel status reported Discord `turn.active: false`, `turn.event: "completed"`, and all four Telegram accounts connected/running. Direct `/readyz` after the proof pass returned `ready:true`, `failing:[]`, and `eventLoop.degraded:false`.
- Observed result after fix: Telegram remained operational, Discord answered the newest observed human message, there were no queued/running task ledger rows, and a follow-up `/readyz` check stayed clean after the heavier channel-status probe.
- What was not tested: No new outbound test messages were sent by the operator; live proof used Andrew's real Discord messages plus read-only Discord REST/status verification.

## Verification
- `CI=true pnpm vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply-reply.config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.runreplyagent.e2e.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run.media-only.test.ts`
- `CI=true pnpm vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.commands.config.ts src/commands/channels.adds-non-default-telegram-account.test.ts`
- `CI=true pnpm vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-discord.config.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.test.ts`
- `CI=true NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false`
- `git diff --check`

target.title: fix(streaming): preserve paragraph separators across block-chunk deliveries [AI-assisted]
target.body:
Fixes #42106

## Summary

The embedded-agent block chunker consumed inter-paragraph `\n\n` separators when
splitting streamed replies on paragraph boundaries. In the `flushOnParagraph`
branch of `EmbeddedBlockChunker.drain()` the emitted chunk was
`source.slice(start, paragraphBreak.index)` — it stopped *at* the `\n\n` and then
advanced `start` past the separator, so the boundary was carried in neither chunk.
A client that reconstructs the visible reply by concatenating successive chunker
outputs got `# TitleFirst paragraph.` instead of `# Title\n\nFirst paragraph.`.

The chunker was originally designed to drop the raw separator on the assumption
that the downstream coalescer always re-inserts it via its `"\n\n"` joiner
(`block-streaming.ts` → `block-reply-coalescer.ts`). That assumption holds only
while multiple chunks stay inside a single coalesced flush; once a flush boundary
lands between two paragraphs (or a consumer reads chunker output directly) the
separator is irrecoverable.

### What changed

1. **`src/agents/embedded-agent-block-chunker.ts`** — the `flushOnParagraph`
   paragraph-break branch now attaches the canonical boundary to the emitted
   chunk: `emit(`${chunk}\n\n`)` instead of `emit(chunk)`. Concatenating the
   chunker's emitted chunks now reconstructs the source faithfully.

2. **`src/auto-reply/reply/block-reply-coalescer.ts`** — mandatory coordination.
   Because a chunk now already ends with `\n\n`, naively re-applying the `"\n\n"`
   joiner would produce `\n\n\n\n`. A new `joinBufferedText` helper collapses the
   seam: when the joiner is itself whitespace it strips a trailing whitespace run
   from the buffered (left) side before joining, leaving exactly one separator
   whether the boundary arrived in-band on the chunk or only via the joiner.
   Both join sites (`mergeBufferedTextWithMedia` and the `enqueue` text-coalescing
   path) use it. Non-whitespace joiners (`" "` sentence preference) are untouched.

### Scope notes

- The `onBlockReply` callback path (`embedded-agent-subscribe.ts` `emitBlockChunk`)
  intentionally `trimEnd()`s each per-paragraph delivery for whitespace-dedup, so
  those per-delivery payloads are unchanged. The recovered separator lives where
  chunks are actually re-joined: the chunker's own emitted output and the coalesced
  outbound wire. The integration test was verified empirically to confirm this
  (its assertions are unchanged), rather than blindly edited.
- No changes to `inbound-debounce-policy.ts` or any WhatsApp/Telegram/channel code.
  Adjacent issues #56145 (non-streaming recombination) and #68986 (visible-text
  sanitization) are out of scope and untouched.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Block streaming dropped the `\n\n` paragraph
separator between successive paragraph-boundary chunk deliveries, so a client that
concatenates streamed chunks rebuilt `# TitleFirst paragraph.` with the heading
glued to the body. After the patch the chunker carries the boundary in-band and
the coalescer collapses the seam, so reconstruction yields `# Title\n\nFirst paragraph.`
with no `\n\n\n\n` regression.

**Real environment tested:** Local clone at HEAD `45d7167e` (branch `r3/fix-42106`),
`node v24.7.0`, `pnpm 11.2.2`, macOS 26.4.1 (Darwin 25.4.0, arm64). In-process
deterministic component test — no network, channel, or external service involved.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** From the repo root, ran the
chunker, integration, and coalescer suites via the repo's vitest runner:
`node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-block-chunker.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-agent-session.streams-soft-chunks-paragraph-preference.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/reply-utils.test.ts`

**Evidence after fix:** Copied live terminal output from running
`node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-block-chunker.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-agent-session.streams-soft-chunks-paragraph-preference.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/reply-utils.test.ts`
against the patched tree. The new reconstruction assertion
`chunks.join("") === "# Title\n\nFirst paragraph."` is the wire-level proof: it is
an in-process deterministic component check (the chunker is pure string→string; no
network/channel involved), so the assertion *is* the observed runtime behavior. The
GREEN run:

```
 RUN  v4.1.8 /private/tmp/oc-r3-wt-42106

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  75 passed (75)   # auto-reply shard (coalescer)

 RUN  v4.1.8 /private/tmp/oc-r3-wt-42106

 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  11 passed (11)   # agents shard (chunker + integration)

[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 5.61s
```

Before the patch (RED), the same `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs` run printed the
dropped separator — actual observed runtime output:

```
 FAIL  src/agents/embedded-agent-block-chunker.test.ts > preserves the paragraph separator across separate flushed chunks (issue #42106)
AssertionError: expected [ '# Title', 'First paragraph.' ] to deeply equal [ '# Title\n\n', 'First paragraph.' ]
- Expected
+ Received
  [
-   "# Title\n\n",
+   "# Title",
    "First paragraph.",
  ]
# chunks.join("") was "# TitleFirst paragraph." (separator dropped on the wire)

 FAIL  src/auto-reply/reply/reply-utils.test.ts > does not emit quadruple newlines when chunks already carry their paragraph boundary (issue #42106)
AssertionError: expected [ 'First paragraph\n\n\n\nSecond paragraph' ] to deeply equal [ 'First paragraph\n\nSecond paragraph' ]
```

**Observed result after fix:** `chunks.join("")` now equals
`"# Title\n\nFirst paragraph."` (separator preserved), and the coalescer emits
exactly `"First paragraph\n\nSecond paragraph"` with no `\n\n\n\n` even when the
incoming chunk already carries its own trailing `\n\n`. All 86 tests across the two
shards pass.

**What was not tested:** Did not exercise an end-to-end delivery through a live
channel transport (WhatsApp/Telegram/Discord) — proof is at the chunker + coalescer
component level on the actual code path, not through a real network round-trip. Did
not fuzz arbitrary paragraph counts beyond the suite's cases (single heading + body,
3-paragraph below-minChars coalescing, fenced-code boundaries, maxChars clamp
splits). Did not measure runtime performance impact (the change is O(1) per chunk).

---

Allow edits by maintainers: yes

AI-assisted disclosure: This change was authored with AI assistance (Claude). A human
reviewed the diff, the RED→GREEN evidence above was produced by running the real test
command against the patched tree, and the author takes responsibility for correctness.

target.title: fix(cron): guard task liveness during startup
target.body:
## Summary

Task registry maintenance now treats cron active-job liveness as non-authoritative until cron startup reconciliation exits. This prevents a pre-start maintenance sweep from marking persisted cron task records lost while the process-local active-job set is still empty after restart.

The change keeps the boundary narrow:

- `activeJobIds` starts non-authoritative after process boot
- `cron.start()` marks liveness reconciling at entry and authoritative in `finally`
- task maintenance preserves only mapped cron tasks while cron liveness is non-authoritative
- once cron start exits, missing active-job state is trusted again

This is AI-assisted.

## Proof

Real setup: local checkout of `openclaw/openclaw`, branch rebased onto `origin/main` at `435e1a74fa`.

Commands run after the rebase/conflict resolution:

```bash
git diff --check
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo
pnpm exec vitest run src/cron/service/ops.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.config.ts
pnpm exec vitest run src/tasks/task-registry.maintenance.issue-60299.test.ts src/tasks/task-registry.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.config.ts
```

Observed result:

- `git diff --check`: passed
- core tsgo: passed
- `src/cron/service/ops.test.ts`: 13 passed
- `src/tasks/task-registry.maintenance.issue-60299.test.ts`: 21 passed
- `src/tasks/task-registry.test.ts`: 63 passed

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Task registry maintenance should preserve mapped stale cron task records while cron active-job liveness is non-authoritative during startup, then resume marking missing mapped cron tasks `lost` after cron startup exits and liveness is authoritative.

**Real setup tested:** Local `openclaw/openclaw` checkout on the PR branch, using an isolated temporary state root under `/tmp/openclaw-pr-80707-proof-*` and the repository's TypeScript runtime via `node --import tsx`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
node --import tsx .artifacts/pr-80707-real-proof.mjs
```

The script seeds 10-minute-stale mapped cron task records with `runtime: cron` and `sourceId`, runs task maintenance once while cron active-job liveness is non-authoritative, then runs task maintenance again after liveness is marked authoritative.

**Evidence after fix:** Redacted terminal output from the isolated local state-dir run:

```json
{
  "stateRoot": "/tmp/openclaw-pr-80707-proof-s9f07s",
  "scenario": "mapped stale cron task maintenance across cron startup liveness authority",
  "staleAgeMs": 600000,
  "beforeStartupExit": {
    "livenessAuthoritative": false,
    "maintenance": {
      "reconciled": 0,
      "recovered": 0,
      "cleanupStamped": 0,
      "pruned": 0
    },
    "task": {
      "runtime": "cron",
      "sourceId": "proof-cron-before-startup-exit",
      "status": "running",
      "endedAtPresent": false
    }
  },
  "afterStartupExit": {
    "livenessAuthoritative": true,
    "maintenance": {
      "reconciled": 2,
      "recovered": 0,
      "cleanupStamped": 0,
      "pruned": 0
    },
    "task": {
      "runtime": "cron",
      "sourceId": "proof-cron-after-startup-exit",
      "status": "lost",
      "endedAtPresent": true,
      "error": "backing session missing"
    }
  }
}
```

**Observed result after fix:** The pre-start/non-authoritative maintenance pass left the stale mapped cron task `running` with `reconciled: 0`. The post-start/authoritative maintenance pass marked the stale mapped cron task `lost` with `error: "backing session missing"`.

**Not tested:** I did not run the full monorepo `pnpm build && pnpm check && pnpm test` locally.

This shows the fixed behavior: task maintenance does not mark a mapped stale cron task lost during the pre-start/non-authoritative window, and does mark mapped stale cron tasks lost after startup liveness becomes authoritative again.

Also ran a focused Codex review against the rebased diff; it reported no blocker. I did not run the full monorepo `pnpm build && pnpm check && pnpm test` locally.

target.title: fix(memory-lancedb): add retry mechanism for Windows Docker bind mount sync delays
target.body:
Hey! 👋

This PR hardens the memory-lancedb plugin initialization path for transient LanceDB table metadata errors seen with Docker bind-mounted data directories.

## The Problem

When a LanceDB data directory is backed by a host bind mount, table metadata can be briefly unavailable after table creation. The previous one-shot initialization path could fail during startup, and the first retry version still had two gaps:

- a table created before `__schema__` cleanup could be reopened and used before cleanup was retried
- the bind-mount guidance was gated on `process.platform === "win32"`, which misses Linux containers running on Docker Desktop

## The Solution

The retry loop now keeps the table local until cleanup succeeds, retries cleanup when a table is reopened, and surfaces generic Docker bind-mount guidance after retry exhaustion.

## What Changed

- Updated `extensions/memory-lancedb/index.ts` to only cache the table after `__schema__` cleanup succeeds.
- Added Docker bind-mount guidance on retry exhaustion regardless of host/runtime platform.
- Added focused coverage in `extensions/memory-lancedb/index.test.ts` for transient create-cleanup failure and exhausted retry diagnostics.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** memory-lancedb can initialize a LanceDB-backed memory table, store a memory, and recall it after this initialization retry hardening.

**Real environment tested:** local OpenClaw plugin registration path using the actual memory-lancedb plugin, actual `@lancedb/lancedb` storage in a temporary database, and a local OpenAI-compatible embeddings endpoint.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```console
$ NODE_OPTIONS=--no-deprecation node --import tsx /tmp/openclaw-memory-lancedb-real-smoke.ts
```

**Evidence after fix:** copied live console output from the real smoke run:

```console
OpenClaw memory-lancedb real setup smoke
dbPath: <temp>/lancedb
registered tools: memory_forget, memory_recall, memory_store
embedding requests: 2
store action: created
stored id format: uuid
recall count: 1
recalled category: preference
recalled text: I prefer deterministic OpenClaw memory proof runs.
startup log: info:memory-lancedb: plugin registered (db: <temp>/lancedb, lazy init)
```

**Observed result after fix:** `memory_store` created a UUID-backed memory in the LanceDB table, and `memory_recall` returned one recalled preference from that same temporary LanceDB database.

**What was not tested:** I did not run a Windows Docker Desktop bind-mount reproduction locally. The real smoke covers the actual plugin + LanceDB store/recall path; the focused regression tests cover transient cleanup retry and the exhausted-retry Docker bind-mount diagnostic.

## Testing

```console
$ pnpm test extensions/memory-lancedb/index.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  46 passed (46)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 12.34s
```

```console
$ pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/memory-lancedb/index.ts extensions/memory-lancedb/index.test.ts
All matched files use the correct format.
```

```console
$ node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/memory-lancedb/index.ts extensions/memory-lancedb/index.test.ts
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
```

```console
$ git diff --check
# no output
```

Fixes #58139
