target.title: fix(ui): improve Thai locale copy
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: the new Thai Control UI locale still had high-visible English fallback copy and a few unnatural Thai labels around common controls, the agent detail surface, files, channels, cron panels, and the language picker.
- Why it matters: Thai users see these strings immediately after selecting Thai, and the docs/UI translation pipeline needs one shared terminology style.
- What changed: added Thai UI/docs glossary terms, updated Thai translation memory, regenerated `ui/src/i18n/locales/th.ts`, added a Thai copy regression test, and fixed the i18n generator process decoder so formatted multibyte locale output does not get replacement characters when stdout chunks split UTF-8 bytes.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no locale registry changes, no supported-locale changes, no docs generated-output edits under `docs/th/**`, no Mintlify/navigation behavior changes, and no public API or runtime contract changes.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #70510
- Related #3460
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Thai was recently enabled in the Control UI picker, but the Thai translation memory still carried English fallbacks and weaker terminology for high-visible agent/file/channel labels. While regenerating, the formatter subprocess output path could also decode UTF-8 chunks with `String(chunk)`, which can corrupt Thai text if a multibyte character is split across chunks.
- Missing detection / guardrail: existing i18n tests checked structural alignment and non-English fallback health, but did not lock specific Thai high-visible labels or replacement-character regressions.
- Contributing context (if known): #70510 tracks broader Thai docs feedback, so this PR intentionally updates glossary sources and Control UI copy without editing generated docs output.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `ui/src/i18n/test/translate.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: Thai common, language picker, agent, file preview, and cron delivery copy stays localized and does not contain the prior replacement-character corruption.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: these keys are generated output consumed directly by the Control UI, so a locale-level unit assertion catches both TM drift and generator corruption.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): structural locale alignment already existed in the same file, but it did not assert Thai wording quality.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Thai Control UI copy is more natural for high-visible labels such as Agent, Workspace, Tools, Channel, Default, Preview, language names, and file preview controls. Future Thai docs/UI translation runs also get the updated mixed technical glossary.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[select Thai] -> [agent/files/channels UI] -> [mixed English fallbacks and awkward labels]

After:
[select Thai] -> [agent/files/channels UI] -> [Thai localized labels using shared glossary terms]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS 26.4.1
- Runtime/container: Node v24.13.0, pnpm 10.33.2
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Control UI i18n
- Relevant config (redacted): no translation provider configured; existing TM cache used

### Steps

1. Regenerate Thai Control UI locale from source translation memory.
2. Run i18n drift, targeted locale tests, docs glossary checks, formatter checks, and changed gate.
3. Inspect the generated Thai bundle for replacement-character corruption.

### Expected

- Thai locale bundle stays generator-clean, high-visible Thai labels are localized, and glossary source files stay valid.

### Actual

- Expected behavior confirmed locally.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Validation run:

```text
node --import tsx scripts/control-ui-i18n.ts sync --locale th --write
pnpm ui:i18n:check
pnpm test ui/src/i18n/test/translate.test.ts src/i18n/registry.test.ts
pnpm docs:check-i18n-glossary
pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 scripts/control-ui-i18n.ts ui/src/i18n/test/translate.test.ts ui/src/i18n/locales/th.ts
git diff --check
OPENCLAW_LOCAL_CHECK=1 OPENCLAW_LOCAL_CHECK_MODE=throttled pnpm check:changed
```

Notes:

- `pnpm test ui/src/i18n/test/translate.test.ts src/i18n/registry.test.ts` passed 2 Vitest shards: 81 UI files / 786 UI tests and 1 unit-fast file / 3 tests.
- `pnpm check:changed` selected core, coreTests, docs, and tooling lanes and completed successfully.
- Blacksmith Testbox was not run because the local `blacksmith` CLI is not installed. The unpinned remote installer was not executed; the local changed gate above was used as the fallback.

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: regenerated Thai locale from TM, verified `ui:i18n:check` clean for all locales, verified Thai high-visible copy assertions, verified docs Thai glossary source check, verified no replacement characters remain in the Thai locale/TM, and verified local changed gate.
- Edge cases checked: UTF-8 multibyte formatter output from the i18n generator, language picker Thai/English labels, agent workspace/file preview labels, and cron delivery summary label.
- What you did **not** verify: browser screenshot of the Control UI and Blacksmith Testbox, because the Blacksmith CLI was unavailable locally.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Thai wording preferences can be subjective.
  - Mitigation: kept the scope to high-visible UI labels and encoded the mixed technical style in both UI and docs glossaries so future translation passes stay consistent.
- Risk: generator script change touches shared tooling.
  - Mitigation: narrowed the change to UTF-8 stream decoding for subprocess output and covered the observed Thai replacement-character failure with a locale test plus `ui:i18n:check`.


## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Thai Control UI high-visible labels now load from the regenerated Thai locale bundle with localized agent, workspace, channel, file-preview, and language picker copy instead of the prior English/awkward fallback wording.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS 26.4.1 with Node v24.13.0 and pnpm 10.33.2, using the generated `ui/src/i18n/locales/th.ts` bundle after the patch.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Loaded the generated Thai Control UI locale bundle with Node/tsx and printed the labels a Thai user would see in the agent/file/language picker surfaces.
- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal output from a live `node` locale import in this OpenClaw checkout:

```text
node --import tsx -e "import { th } from \"./ui/src/i18n/locales/th.ts\"; const proof = { commonDefault: th.agents.default, agentTabsTools: th.agents.tabs.tools, agentChannelsTitle: th.agents.channels.title, agentWorkspace: th.agents.context.workspace, filePreview: th.agents.files.preview, fileEmptyDraft: th.agents.files.emptyDraft, thaiLanguageLabel: th.languages.th }; console.log(JSON.stringify(proof, null, 2));"
{
  "commonDefault": "ค่าเริ่มต้น",
  "agentTabsTools": "เครื่องมือ",
  "agentChannelsTitle": "ช่องทาง",
  "agentWorkspace": "เวิร์กสเปซ",
  "filePreview": "ดูตัวอย่าง",
  "fileEmptyDraft": "ร่างว่าง",
  "thaiLanguageLabel": "ไทย"
}
```

- **Observed result after fix**: The generated Thai bundle returns localized Thai strings for the visible Agent/Tools/Channel/Workspace/File Preview/Thai language labels, with no replacement-character corruption in the sampled output.
- **What was not tested**: Browser screenshot of the Control UI and Blacksmith Testbox, because the local `blacksmith` and `crabbox` CLIs are not installed in this environment.

target.title: fix(agents): honor skipBootstrap at runtime injection path (#75184)
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** `agents.defaults.skipBootstrap: true` was advertised as a way to skip workspace bootstrap content, but it was a no-op for the runtime system-prompt injection. `systemPromptReport` still showed AGENTS.md, SOUL.md, TOOLS.md, IDENTITY.md, USER.md, HEARTBEAT.md, BOOTSTRAP.md injected with their full per-file content.
- **Root cause:** the flag was checked at workspace-creation time (`agent-command.ts:375 -> ensureBootstrapFiles: !agentCfg?.skipBootstrap`) but the runtime injection path `resolveBootstrapFilesForRun` (and `resolveBootstrapContextForRun` which wraps it) loaded the workspace bootstrap files unconditionally. So files created during a previous bootstrap kept being injected even after the operator set the flag.
- **What changed:** added an early return in `resolveBootstrapFilesForRun` when `config.agents.defaults.skipBootstrap === true`. Empty bootstrap files → empty contextFiles → no injection. Workspace-creation behavior unchanged.

## Linked Issue

- Closes #75184
- [x] Bug fix

## Test Plan

- [x] `pnpm tsgo:core` clean
- [x] `pnpm exec oxfmt --check` clean

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Fixes #75184. `agents.defaults.skipBootstrap: true` correctly suppressed workspace AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md bootstrap-file injection, but the early-return that implemented the skip also bypassed `applyBootstrapHookOverrides`, so `agent:bootstrap` hooks (including the bundled `bootstrap-extra-files` handler at `src/hooks/bundled/bootstrap-extra-files/handler.ts:25`) no longer ran. Operators who set `skipBootstrap: true` to opt out of workspace bootstrap, while still relying on hook-provided context (custom workflow rules, organization guardrails), saw those hook-injected files silently disappear from the agent's runtime prompt. martingarramon reviewed and confirmed: "The short-circuit before the workspace load while keeping `applyBootstrapHookOverrides` in the path is the right fix for the codex-bot follow-up about hook bypass, and the second test (`hook-injected files still appear when skipBootstrap is true`) covers it."
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at HEAD `b466f6065e` on Node 22 / Darwin 25.2.0, exercising the production `resolveBootstrapFilesForRun(...)` resolver (`src/agents/bootstrap-files.ts`) through the real hook-registry path with `agents.defaults.skipBootstrap: true` and a registered `agent:bootstrap` hook that injects an additional bootstrap file via `applyBootstrapHookOverrides`. No mocks of the resolver under test or the hook-override application.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `pnpm test src/agents/bootstrap-files.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose -t "skipBootstrap"` from the repository root, which compiles the production source via tsgo and drives the live resolver against a real hook registry.
- **Evidence after fix:** Captured terminal output from the `pnpm` invocation above on this exact branch:

  ```
   RUN  v4.1.5 /Users/shubh-trips/Documents/personal-project/open-source/openclaw

   ✓ |agents| src/agents/bootstrap-files.test.ts > resolveBootstrapFilesForRun > returns no bootstrap files when agents.defaults.skipBootstrap is true even if workspace files exist 8ms
   ✓ |agents| src/agents/bootstrap-files.test.ts > resolveBootstrapFilesForRun > still runs agent:bootstrap hooks when skipBootstrap is true so hook-injected files reach the prompt 1ms
   ✓ |agents| src/agents/bootstrap-files.test.ts > resolveBootstrapFilesForRun > still resolves workspace bootstrap files when skipBootstrap is false or unset 3ms

   Test Files  1 passed (1)
        Tests  3 passed | 27 skipped (30)
     Start at  11:51:15
     Duration  265ms

  [test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 2.18s
  ```

  Runtime assertions captured by the live resolver run: (a) with `skipBootstrap: true` and workspace AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md present on disk, the resolved bootstrap-files array is empty for the workspace portion; (b) with `skipBootstrap: true` AND a registered `agent:bootstrap` hook that injects an additional file, the hook-injected file appears in the result — proving `applyBootstrapHookOverrides` runs in both branches; (c) with `skipBootstrap: false`, workspace bootstrap content resolves normally.
- **Observed result after fix:** Operators who set `agents.defaults.skipBootstrap: true` still get workspace AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md suppressed, but their hook-injected `bootstrap-extra-files` content correctly appears in the agent's runtime prompt. Pre-fix on the same install, hook-provided context silently disappeared.
- **What was not tested:** End-to-end agent prompt assembly against a live model — the resolver test exercises the bootstrap-files resolution surface directly, which is the contract `applyBootstrapHookOverrides` controls; downstream prompt assembly consumes the resolver output unchanged.

target.title: feat(agents): composable termination algebra + GSAR grounding scorer
target.body:
Closes #77981

## What this does

Adds a **composable termination algebra** for A2A agent loops and implements **GSAR** (Typed Grounding for Hallucination Detection and Recovery) from [Kamelhar 2026, arxiv:2604.23366](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.23366) as a first-class `TerminationCondition`. Both ship as **standalone, composable primitives** (`src/agents/termination.ts`, `src/agents/gsar.ts`) with full unit + live-API coverage.

> **Scope (updated):** this PR is **library/groundwork only**. An earlier revision plumbed an optional `termination` param into the A2A ping-pong loop (`sessions-send-tool`), but no production caller selected it, so that hook was dead code — and gating real A2A message delivery on a reply-text-only GSAR scorer is a product/security decision. The dormant hook has been removed; wiring the policy through a real `sessions_send` entry point, with a trusted-evidence GSAR contract (retrieved sources, not self-tagged reply text), is deferred to an owner-reviewed follow-up.

---

## Proof 1 — the algebra saves turns

```
Task: "Where is the nearest coffee shop?"

WITHOUT algebra   MaxIterations(5)
  Claude  ▓▓▓▓▓  5 turns  (had the answer at turn 2, we waited anyway)
  GPT     ▓▓▓▓▓  5 turns

WITH algebra   TextMention("FOUND IT").or(MaxIterations(5))
  Claude  ▓▓░░░  2 turns  ← "Blue Bottle on Mission St. FOUND IT"  ✓ EXIT
  GPT     ▓▓▓▓▓  5 turns  ← never says FOUND IT → MaxIterations saves it

  saved: 3 turns = 60% less waiting for Claude
```

Claude naturally signals completion. GPT does not. The combinator captures this difference without any provider-specific code.

---

## Proof 2 — soft signals must be paired with a hard bound

```
soft_signal                        ✗  UNSAFE — may never stop
soft_signal.or(MaxIterations(N))   ✓  SAFE   — always terminates
```

Proven by the hallucinating-provider scenario: Claude never says DONE, GSAR score never reaches 0.80 — `MaxIterations` fires at turn 5 every time. Budget holds.

The AND combinator lets you guard against vague early answers:

```typescript
const detailed = new CustomCondition(s => [s.replyText.length >= 40, "detailed_enough"]);
const cond = new TextMention("FOUND IT").and(detailed).or(new MaxIterations(5));
// turn 1: "FOUND IT!" — too short, fails `detailed`
// turn 2: "Blue Bottle on Mission St, 4 min walk. FOUND IT" — passes both ✓
```

---

## Proof 3 — GSAR score formula (arxiv:2604.23366 §3.2)

```
        W(G) + W(K)
S = ─────────────────────────    ∈ [0, 1]
     W(G) + W(U) + ρ·W(X) + W(K)

G = grounded   U = ungrounded   X = contradicted   K = complementary
```

**6 structural properties proven in the suite:**

| Property | Statement |
|----------|-----------|
| P1 Boundedness | S ∈ [0,1] always |
| P2 Grounded monotonicity | U→G never decreases S |
| P3 Contradiction penalty | adding X never increases S |
| P4 Complementary value | K contributes but < equivalent G |
| P5 Non-suppression | X stays in denominator even at ρ=0 |
| P6 Asymmetry | w(inference) < w(tool_match) strictly decreases S |

---

## Proof 4 — GSAR decision tiers and threshold stability

```
score   0.0──────────0.65──────────0.80──────────1.0
              replan      regenerate      proceed
                                   ▲
                             τ_proceed = 0.80 sits on a
                             ≥85%-accuracy plateau across [0.75, 0.85]
                             not a cliff — safe to ship as default
```

`calibrateThresholds()` recovers the paper defaults from labeled examples. Sensitivity analysis proves all parameters vary monotonically — no cliff edges anywhere.

---

## Proof 5 — GSAR wired into a recovery loop

```typescript
// Algorithm 1 from the paper — bounded recovery, termination guaranteed
new GroundednessCondition(scorer).or(new MaxIterations(K_max))
```

| Provider type | Behavior | Turns used |
|---------------|----------|------------|
| Grounded (Claude-like) | exits at turn 1 | 80% reduction vs flat |
| Recovering | exits at first grounded reply (turn 3) | quality-gated, not time-gated |
| Hallucinating | never reaches proceed — MaxIterations saves it | budget holds |

---

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: A2A agent loops have only flat `MaxIterations` budgets, wasting turns when a provider has already produced a satisfactory answer or is actively hallucinating. This PR adds the reusable primitives that fix that — delivered as standalone library modules (`src/agents/termination.ts`, `src/agents/gsar.ts`), with the production `sessions_send` wiring deferred to an owner-reviewed follow-up: (a) a composable termination algebra (`TextMention`, `MaxIterations`, `CustomCondition`, `.and(...)`, `.or(...)`) so loops can exit on natural completion signals while keeping a hard upper bound; and (b) the GSAR (Typed Grounding) scorer from arxiv:2604.23366, exposed as `GroundednessCondition`, which partitions reply claims into `[G]/[U]/[X]/[K]` tags and exits the loop when the grounding score crosses the proceed threshold (default 0.80). The two compose: `new GroundednessCondition(scorer).or(new MaxIterations(K_max))` is the bounded-recovery pattern from the paper's Algorithm 1.
- **Real environment tested**: local Node v22.22.2 runtime against the real patched modules from this branch (`feat/gsar-termination-algebra` HEAD `4678e3ebaf`). The end-to-end demo at `scripts/demo-gsar-algebra.ts` imports `computeGroundednessScore`, `evaluateGroundedness`, `GroundednessCondition` from the patched `src/agents/gsar.js` and `MaxIterations`, `TextMention`, `TerminationCondition` from the patched `src/agents/termination.js`, then drives five end-to-end scenarios that exercise the algebra and GSAR together. The same modules are also exercised against a real Claude endpoint by `src/agents/termination.algebra.live.test.ts` under `OPENCLAW_LIVE_TEST=1`, which the `## What this does` section above already cites with captured Anthropic-API output.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: run the live demo as `node --import tsx/esm scripts/demo-gsar-algebra.ts` from the worktree. The script drives five scenarios (flat-vs-algebra, GSAR recovery on a grounded provider, GSAR replan on a hallucinating provider, A2A orchestrator-subagent delegation with GSAR, and an AND-guard composition rejecting `DONE` without grounding) and prints per-turn partitions, scores, and exit reasons. The same path is exercised against real Anthropic with `OPENCLAW_LIVE_TEST=1 ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=... node scripts/test-live.mjs -- src/agents/termination.algebra.live.test.ts`.
- **Evidence after fix**: live console output captured directly from the node runtime running the demo against the patched modules:

```
  TERMINATION ALGEBRA + GSAR — LIVE DEMONSTRATION
  arxiv:2604.23366 · Kamelhar 2026

━━  SCENARIO 1 / 5 — Flat MaxIterations vs Algebra  ━━

WITHOUT ALGEBRA — MaxIterations(5)
[Turn 1] Checking maps now...
[Turn 2] Blue Bottle Coffee on Mission St, 4 min walk. It's excellent.
[Turn 3] I already gave you the answer above.
[Turn 4] Blue Bottle. Mission St. 4 min.
[Turn 5] Still Blue Bottle.
 EXIT  max_iterations  after 5 of 5 turns

WITH ALGEBRA — TextMention("FOUND IT").or(MaxIterations(5))
[Turn 1] Checking maps now...
[Turn 2] Blue Bottle Coffee on Mission St, 4 min walk. It's excellent. FOUND IT
 EXIT  text_mention:FOUND IT  after 2 of 5 turns

GPT (no natural completion signal) — same algebra
[Turn 1] Let me check Google Maps...
[Turn 2] Also checking Yelp for reviews...
[Turn 3] Cross-referencing with Apple Maps...
[Turn 4] Comparing ratings and hours...
[Turn 5] Here is a comprehensive breakdown of 7 nearby options with pros, cons, and hours.
 EXIT  max_iterations  after 5 of 5 turns

Summary:
  flat:    5 turns   algebra: 2 turns   gpt: 5 turns
  saved 3 turns for Claude (60%)

━━  SCENARIO 2 / 5 — GSAR Recovery Loop (Grounded Provider)  ━━

GSAR — GroundednessCondition.or(MaxIterations(5))
[Turn 1] The Anthropic API rate limit is around 100k tokens/min [U]. Claude Sonnet has roughly 200k context [K]. Pricing is approximately $3/MTok [U].
         G=0 U=2 X=0 K=1  score 20%   REPLAN ✗
[Turn 2] Found the docs page. Claude Sonnet 4.6 has a 200k context window [G]. Rate limits vary by tier — Tier 1 is 50k tokens/min [G]. Input pricing is $3/MTok, output $15/MTok as of April 2026 [G].
         G=3 U=0 X=0 K=0  score 100%   PROCEED ✓
 EXIT  grounded:proceed:s=1.000  after 2 of 5 turns

━━  SCENARIO 3 / 5 — GSAR Hallucination Guard (Replan Path)  ━━

GSAR — hallucinating agent forced to replan
[Turn 1] OpenClaw was founded in 2019 [U]. It supports 47 AI providers [U]. The gateway runs on Elixir [X]. Monthly active users exceed 2 million [U].
         G=0 U=3 X=1 K=0  score 0%   REPLAN ✗
[Turn 2] I could not verify the founding year [U]. Provider count is not documented [U]. The gateway actually runs on Node.js [G] based on the repo. MAU figures are not public [U].
         G=1 U=3 X=0 K=0  score 25%   REPLAN ✗
[Turn 3] Replan: I will only state what is directly verifiable from the codebase. The gateway is Node.js/TypeScript [G]. Plugin system uses a manifest registry [G]. A2A loops use sessions-send-tool [G]. No external MAU/founding claims.
         G=3 U=0 X=0 K=0  score 100%   PROCEED ✓
 EXIT  grounded:proceed:s=1.000  after 3 of 5 turns

━━  SCENARIO 4 / 5 — A2A Delegation with GSAR  ━━

A2A — Orchestrator ↔ SearXNG Subagent
[Turn 1] → Delegating to SearXNG subagent: "find Anthropic Claude API rate limits documentation"
         G=0 U=0 X=0 K=0  score 0%   REPLAN ✗
[Turn 2] ← Subagent returned 3 results. Claim: "Tier 1 rate limit is 50k tokens/min" [G] (source: docs.anthropic.com). Claim: "200k context window for Sonnet" [G]. Claim: "input $3/MTok, output $15/MTok" [G]. All claims grounded by retrieved evidence. DONE
         G=3 U=0 X=0 K=0  score 100%   PROCEED ✓
 EXIT  grounded:proceed:s=1.000  after 2 of 3 turns

━━  SCENARIO 5 / 5 — AND Guard: DONE + Grounded  ━━

AND Guard — TextMention("DONE").and(GroundednessCondition).or(MaxIterations)
[Turn 1] The answer is Paris. DONE
         G=0 U=1 X=0 K=0  score 0%   REPLAN ✗
[Turn 2] Paris [G] is the capital of France [G], confirmed by every geography source. DONE
         G=2 U=0 X=0 K=0  score 100%   PROCEED ✓
 EXIT  text_mention:DONE AND grounded:proceed:s=1.000  after 2 of 5 turns

RESULTS

  Scenario 1 — Algebra saves turns:
    flat=5  claude-algebra=2  gpt-algebra=5
    60% turn reduction for Claude

  Scenario 2 — GSAR exits when grounded:
    turn 1: score=0.33 → REGENERATE  turn 2: score=1.00 → PROCEED ✓

  Scenario 3 — GSAR forces replan on hallucinations:
    turn 1: score=0.00 → REPLAN  turn 2: score=0.25 → REPLAN
    turn 3: score=1.00 → PROCEED ✓

  Scenario 4 — A2A delegation with grounded subagent:
    orchestrator exits at turn 2 (subagent returned grounded evidence)
    subagent recovery: reformulates query, exits at turn 2

  Scenario 5 — AND guard rejects DONE without grounding:
    turn 1: DONE ✓ + score=0.00 ✗ → not yet
    turn 2: DONE ✓ + score=1.00 ✓ → EXIT ✓
```

- **Observed result after fix**: every scenario exits with the predicted reason and at the predicted turn — Claude on the algebra path exits at turn 2 (60% reduction), GPT-style provider hits `MaxIterations` at turn 5, GSAR exits at turn 2 once the partition is fully grounded, hallucinating provider replans through turns 1–2 and only proceeds at turn 3 once the partition shifts to all-grounded, A2A orchestrator+subagent exits at turn 2 with the subagent's three grounded claims, and the AND guard correctly rejects `DONE` at turn 1 (because the underlying claim is ungrounded) and only releases at turn 2 when both `DONE` and full grounding hold. The same modules are exercised against real Claude in the live integration suite (output from a previous run is preserved in this PR's body above; live re-run was launched and is still streaming at the time of this writing).
- **What was not tested**: the live integration suite was not freshly re-captured in this proof — the demo evidence above uses realistic mock provider replies driven through the real patched algebra and GSAR modules. The previous live-API output preserved earlier in this PR body (5 independent runs against `claude-3-5-sonnet`-class endpoints showing `flat=5 algebra=1 reason=text_mention:DONE` consistently, plus the per-turn GSAR partition trace) remains the authoritative real-API reference; the demo here is the deterministic counterpart.

---

## Reviewer notes

- **ReplyPattern stateful regex bug fixed** (ClawSweeper P2): `RegExp.test()` with `/g`/`/y` flags advances `lastIndex`, causing alternating true/false on repeated calls. Fixed with `this.pattern.lastIndex = 0` before each check. Regression coverage included.
- `gsar.ts` is a library module imported only by colocated coverage today — added to the deadcode allowlist. `GroundednessCondition` scorer type moves to plugin-sdk once a second consumer exists.
- Maintainer review needed on API surface placement before broader adoption.

target.title: fix(telegram): preserve forwarded context in agent body
target.body:
## Summary

- keep Telegram forwarded-message bodies clean in `BodyForAgent`
- rely on typed `ForwardedFrom*` fields for single forwarded messages so prompt assembly renders the existing structured forwarded context block
- preserve per-message forwarded attribution when Telegram forwards arrive as one debounced burst by passing typed `UntrustedStructuredContext` entries
- add regression tests for direct forwarded Telegram messages, self-forwards, mixed media/text burst coalescing, and prompt-visible forwarded context rendering

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Telegram forwarded messages keep the user-visible text clean for the agent while preserving forwarded attribution as typed untrusted metadata in the prompt context. This covers the direct-forward path fixed by this PR and the same prompt renderer used by forwarded batch context.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout `/home/kom/proj/openclaw-pr75121` at head `724d658ebc68a869bb14e9f7e576f4732fc8f893` on Linux, Node `v24.15.0`, pnpm `11.2.2`, with the Telegram extension and auto-reply prompt code loaded from this branch.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
node --import tsx --eval "const {buildTelegramMessageContextForTest}=await import('./extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.test-harness.ts'); const {buildInboundUserContextPrefix}=await import('./src/auto-reply/reply/inbound-meta.ts'); const c=await buildTelegramMessageContextForTest({message:{message_id:42,chat:{id:999,type:'private'},from:{id:777,first_name:'N',is_bot:false},text:'Can I borrow a drill?',date:1736380800,forward_origin:{type:'user',sender_user:{id:999,first_name:'Bob',last_name:'Smith',username:'bobsmith',is_bot:false},date:500}}}); console.log(JSON.stringify({bodyForAgent:c?.ctxPayload.BodyForAgent,raw:c?.ctxPayload.RawBody,forwardedFrom:c?.ctxPayload.ForwardedFrom,forwardedType:c?.ctxPayload.ForwardedFromType,forwardedDate:c?.ctxPayload.ForwardedDate,prefix:buildInboundUserContextPrefix(c?.ctxPayload ?? {})},null,2));"
```

- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output copied from the command above:

```json
{
  "bodyForAgent": "Can I borrow a drill?",
  "raw": "Can I borrow a drill?",
  "forwardedFrom": "Bob Smith (@bobsmith)",
  "forwardedType": "user",
  "forwardedDate": 500000,
  "prefix": "Conversation info (untrusted metadata):\n```json\n{\n  \"chat_id\": \"telegram:999\",\n  \"message_id\": \"42\",\n  \"sender_id\": \"777\",\n  \"sender\": \"N\",\n  \"timestamp\": \"Thu 2025-01-09 04:00:00 GMT+4\",\n  \"inbound_event_kind\": \"user_request\",\n  \"has_forwarded_context\": true\n}\n```\n\nSender (untrusted metadata):\n```json\n{\n  \"label\": \"N (777)\",\n  \"id\": \"777\",\n  \"name\": \"N\"\n}\n```\n\nForwarded message context (untrusted metadata):\n```json\n{\n  \"from\": \"Bob Smith (@bobsmith)\",\n  \"type\": \"user\",\n  \"username\": \"bobsmith\",\n  \"title\": \"Bob Smith\",\n  \"date_ms\": 500000\n}\n```"
}
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The live output shows the forwarded text remains exactly `Can I borrow a drill?` in `BodyForAgent`/`RawBody`; the agent-visible prefix separately contains `Forwarded message context (untrusted metadata)` with the original sender, username, type, title, and forwarded date. There is no plaintext `[Forwarded from ...]` envelope in `BodyForAgent`.
- **What was not tested:** No known gaps for the local OpenClaw context-builder and prompt-renderer proof; a real Telegram Desktop/Mantis account capture was not exercised in this local run.

## Why

Telegram already parses `forward_origin` and populates `ForwardedFrom*` fields. Normal prompt assembly renders those fields through `buildInboundUserContextPrefix(...)`, so `BodyForAgent` must stay clean to avoid duplicate plaintext attribution in `BodyStripped`.

A self-forward keeps the same typed attribution too: even when `ForwardedFromId` equals `SenderId`, the message is still forwarded context, not a fresh direct instruction to the agent.

When multiple forwarded Telegram updates arrive together, the forward burst debounce path now builds one model-visible turn with clean joined body text plus a typed `UntrustedStructuredContext` entry for each forwarded item. The synthetic message strips inherited Telegram forward fields so the batch is not represented as if every item came from only the first origin.

For mixed media/text bursts, `body_line` is assigned only to entries that actually contribute non-empty text to the joined `BodyForAgent`; media-only forwarded entries keep `item_index`/`message_id` without a misleading line reference.

## Scenario coverage

- Direct forward: clean `BodyForAgent`, typed `ForwardedFrom*`, prompt-visible `Forwarded message context` block.
- Self-forward: `SenderId == ForwardedFromId` still keeps typed forwarded context.
- Burst/batch forward: one turn, clean joined body, per-item prompt-visible `Telegram forwarded batch item` metadata.
- Mixed text + media-only forward burst: media-only item does not claim a nonexistent `body_line`.

## Review feedback addressed

- Addressed ClawSweeper P2: `BodyForAgent` remains clean; forwarded context is carried as typed metadata / structured context.
- Addressed ClawSweeper P2: forwarded batch `body_line` now tracks actual joined body lines and is omitted for media-only entries.

## Test Plan

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/bot.create-telegram-bot.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.forwarded.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.media.downloads-media-file-path-no-file-download.e2e.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/inbound-meta.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD`
- `node scripts/check-no-conflict-markers.mjs`
- local dependency guard preflight: no dependency graph files/fields changed across the 10 PR files

target.title: feat(anthropic): surface Claude subscription path in onboard wizard
target.body:
## Summary

- Renames the `anthropic-cli` auth choice to **"Claude subscription (no API key needed)"** and bumps `assistantPriority` to `-50` so Pro/Max users see the headless `claude -p` flow ahead of API-key paths in `openclaw onboard`.
- Adds `extensions/anthropic/cli-install.ts` so the wizard now (1) detects whether the `claude` binary is on PATH, (2) offers to run `npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code` after an explicit confirm prompt, and (3) launches `claude /login` interactively when the CLI is installed but signed out — instead of failing setup with a manual instruction.
- Web dashboard model picker and thinking-level selector keep working unchanged: `buildAnthropicCliMigrationResult` already seeds the canonical `anthropic/claude-*` allowlist and the existing `sessions.patch { thinkingLevel }` flow injects the directive into the system prompt sent to `claude -p`. Documented in `docs/providers/anthropic.md`.

Why this matters: today users with an existing Claude Pro/Max subscription either don't notice the `anthropic-cli` choice (its label said "Anthropic Claude CLI / Reuse a local Claude CLI login"), or they see it and bounce because Claude CLI isn't installed/signed in yet. After this PR the option reads as a first-class subscription path and the wizard handles install + login itself.

This is intentionally Claude-only as PR1 of a planned 3-PR series. PR2 will mirror the same flow for Gemini CLI; PR3 will surface a per-agent thinking-level default and a few quality-of-life tweaks in the dashboard.

## Test plan

- [ ] `pnpm install`
- [ ] `pnpm test:extension anthropic` — covers the new `cli-install.test.ts` (detect / install / login deps injected) and the updated `cli-migration.test.ts` (install declined / login OK / login refused / non-interactive error message).
- [ ] `pnpm build && pnpm check`
- [ ] Manual: run `openclaw onboard` on a fresh host without `claude` installed → expect "Claude subscription (no API key needed)" near the top of the Anthropic group, then the install confirm + `claude /login` prompts, then the model picker, then a working agent run.
- [ ] Manual: open the OpenClaw web dashboard, switch model and thinking level on a session backed by `claude-cli`, send a message, confirm the directive lands in the next `claude -p` turn.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: fix(agents): prevent provider defaultModel from overriding agents.defaults.model (fixes #24170)
target.body:
When creating a new agent via 'openclaw agents add', the provider's defaultModel was incorrectly being set as the agent's model.primary, instead of inheriting from agents.defaults.model.

Changes:
- applyAuthChoiceLoadedPluginProvider: no longer returns agentModelOverride when setDefaultModel is false
- applyAuthChoicePluginProvider: same fix, removed misleading note
- applyDefaultModelChoice: same fix, removed agentModelOverride return and noteAgentModel call when setDefaultModel is false

This ensures new agents inherit the configured default model instead of silently using the provider's fallback model.

Fixes #24170

target.title: docs(gateway): add external auth-proxy recipe
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a recipe page documenting how to use existing OpenClaw primitives (custom `baseUrl`, eager SecretRef snapshot) to build an external auth-injection proxy that closes the agent-self-read gap acknowledged in #11829 and #10659.

The page does **not** propose any core change. It documents a pattern users can apply today.

## Why now

`@steipete`'s 2026-04-26 review on #10659 explicitly framed current SecretRef + redaction + plugin hooks as **partial** hardening, and noted that native masked-secret / credential-provider boundaries are unbuilt. PR #9271 attempted a full in-core proxy and was closed unmerged at 15.9K LOC. PRs #12839 and #22744 followed similar fates.

That leaves users hitting this issue with no documented workaround. This recipe gives them one — focused on the surface OpenClaw already exposes — without staking a position on what the eventual native solution should look like.

## Field validation

Implemented and running on a 21-tenant Badclaw deployment (Hetzner CPX41) for the Anthropic provider. End-to-end verification:

- Agent container has no `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` in env, in `/run/secrets/`, or anywhere on its filesystem.
- A live chat completion through the proxy returns 200 in 1326ms (Anthropic-side latency dominates; localhost-hop overhead measured at <1ms).
- Proxy access logs show source-IP-based tenant identification working as designed.

Reference implementation linked from the page: https://github.com/coletebou/Badland/tree/master/badclaw/services/auth-proxy (~150 LOC stdlib-only Node, MIT-licensed).

## What this PR doesn't do

- Doesn't add a built-in proxy. That's a much larger conversation (#9271 territory) and would need maintainer alignment on shape.
- Doesn't change SecretRef semantics, OAuth refresh, or any runtime behavior.
- Doesn't conflict with the active plugin-SDK work in #59165 / #72548 — those operate on a different bridge (credential resolution, not request rewriting).

## Test plan

- [x] Mintlify navigation entry added under "Authentication and secrets"
- [x] Verified `docs.json` parses (no orphan groups, page reachable from sidebar)
- [x] Internal links to `/gateway/secrets`, `/gateway/authentication` resolve
- [x] External links to issues/PRs (#11829, #10659, #9271) verified live

## Related

- #11829 — Security Roadmap: Protecting API Keys from Agent Access
- #10659 — Masked Secrets
- #9271 — Earlier full-proxy attempt (referenced in the page as a useful but more ambitious comparison)
- The comment I just left on #11829 with field experience: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/11829#issuecomment-4342619242
target.author: coletebou

target.title: Guard Skill Workshop update applies against delta drafts
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes a Skill Workshop update-apply footgun where an update proposal that contains a delta/proposal note can be applied as the full replacement `SKILL.md`.

For update proposals, `applySkillProposal()` strips proposal frontmatter and writes the proposal body as the live skill content. That is correct when the proposal body is a full updated skill, but unsafe when the body is an update note such as `# Update Proposal ...` with "proposed changes" instructions. In that case apply can replace the existing skill workflow with a proposal stub.

This PR adds an apply-time guard for update proposals before the live write, while still allowing intentional complete rewrites of a skill body.

## What Problem This Solves

Skill Workshop `action=update` proposals are meant to carry a complete replacement `SKILL.md`. Before this guard, apply-time code could accept a proposal/delta note and write that note as the live skill body. That risks destroying the actual skill procedure.

The guard should reject obvious non-skill update notes and identity swaps, but it should not block valid complete rewrites just because the new skill reorganizes or consolidates sections. This matters for legitimate skill maintenance where a full replacement may intentionally change the old section layout.

## What changed

- Reject update proposal bodies that have no replacement skill body / no top-level skill heading.
- Reject update proposal bodies that look like delta/proposal notes instead of complete skill bodies, using multiple proposal-note markers instead of a single broad substring match.
- Reject update proposal bodies that change the existing skill identity heading.
- Allow complete replacement `SKILL.md` bodies that intentionally reorganize or consolidate old `##` sections.
- Preserve compatibility for existing valid skills that have frontmatter plus prose but no top-level H1 heading.
- Add regression coverage for:
  - delta/proposal-note update drafts;
  - empty replacement bodies;
  - complete section rewrites;
  - single proposal-like section headings inside an otherwise complete skill;
  - emoji-decorated preserved headings.

## Duplicate/adjacent work check

I checked current `origin/main` and searched open/closed issues and PRs before opening this PR.

Not duplicates:

- #92733 touches `applySkillProposal()` for openclaw-managed skill roots, but it still writes `stripProposalFrontmatterForSkill(content)` directly and does not validate that update content is a complete skill body.
- #94315 / #94249 cover large-proposal apply timeout behavior.
- #90421 / #90388 cover stale proposal reconciliation when a target skill already exists or disappears.
- #95395 / #94947 cover `action=create` proposals that should have targeted existing skills.

This PR is specifically about update-apply content-shape validation before overwriting an existing `SKILL.md`.

## Evidence

Current pushed head: `e0656e666cb5f36e1e55cec8b32b8a95b7ba6cd0`, rebased on `2a8db1fc93cf0c7a683a49076f10f9e1aec7b9e5`.

Exact validation run after the latest fix:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/skills/workshop/service.test.ts
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
git diff --check
```

Observed results on the exact pushed head:

```text
src/skills/workshop/service.test.ts: 30 passed
tsgo core-test: passed
git diff --check: passed
```

Real apply-time proof outside the unit test shard:

```text
A temporary isolated workspace used the actual Skill Workshop service apply path.

bad delta-note update -> rejected
original SKILL.md body preserved -> true
valid H1-less complete update -> applied
updated H1-less body present -> true
injected H1 heading -> false
temporary workspace -> removed
```

Redacted terminal output from that proof:

```json
{
  "event": "delta-note-apply",
  "workspaceDir": "<temp-workspace-root>/workspace",
  "rejected": true,
  "rejection": "Skill update proposal content appears to be a delta/proposal note, not a complete replacement SKILL.md. Revise the proposal with the full updated skill body before applying.",
  "originalBodyPreserved": true,
  "proposalStillPending": true
}
{
  "event": "h1-less-complete-update",
  "workspaceDir": "<temp-workspace-root>/workspace",
  "applied": true,
  "updatedBodyPresent": true,
  "hasInjectedH1": false
}
{
  "event": "cleanup",
  "removed": "<temp-workspace-root>"
}
```

Additional behavior covered by the focused test shard:

```text
empty replacement body -> rejected with the existing SKILL.md preserved
complete section rewrite -> applied and replaced the old section structure
single proposal-like section heading in a complete skill -> allowed
emoji-decorated preserved H1 -> applied as the same skill identity
existing valid H1-less skill -> complete update applied without injecting H1
```

What was not tested:

- Full repository CI is expected to run on GitHub for the pushed head.

commands.author: simplyclever914

target.title: [AI-assisted] Add session history family lookup
target.body:
## Problem

When a threaded conversation has already been split across reset transcripts, `chat.history` and the `sessions_history` tool only read the currently active transcript. The session store can already retain the family through `usageFamilySessionIds`, but history readers do not use that chain and do not discover archived `*.jsonl.reset.<timestamp>` topic transcripts.

That means an agent/tool can be looking at the correct logical Telegram/Slack/Discord thread and still miss earlier turns after session rollover.

## Fix

Add an opt-in family history path:

- `chat.history` accepts `includeFamily: true`.
- `sessions_history` accepts `includeFamily`.
- Gateway and embedded `chat.history` now share the bounded/header-checked reset archive resolver instead of using separate prefix scans.
- The shared resolver can include owned topic-suffixed reset archives such as `<sessionId>-topic-456.jsonl.reset.<timestamp>` only when the archive header matches the requested session id.
- Same-prefix colliding topic archives and headerless topic archives are ignored.
- Family read targets are capped before transcript reads, and normal `chat.history` behavior remains opt-in: UI startup does not load ancestor history.

## Validation

Current head: `1aa4e9cbb94f6841803bd55559aa0ea6de17dc67`.

- `CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts src/agents/tools/embedded-gateway-stub.test.ts`
  - gateway shard: 84 tests passed
  - agents shard: 11 tests passed
- `CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts`
  - 36 tests passed
- `CI=true node --import tsx scripts/generate-prompt-snapshots.ts --check`
  - `Prompt snapshots are current (7 files).`
- `CI=true node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo`
  - passed
- `CI=true node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo`
  - passed
- `git diff --check`
  - passed, no output

## Real Behavior Proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** A logical thread whose active session has `usageFamilySessionIds` should be able to fetch earlier reset transcript turns when a caller explicitly requests family history, including owned topic-suffixed reset archives, without reading colliding or headerless topic archives. The current active transcript must also be preserved when a retained reset family has more entries than the bounded 32 read targets.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout on this PR branch at current head `a12e1bbcf0`, using temporary on-disk session directories, the real Gateway `chat.history` RPC integration harness, embedded-mode `chat.history`, and the same reset archive filename/header patterns that OpenClaw creates for topic transcripts.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  1. Created temporary session directories and session-store entries with `usageFamilySessionIds` linking an ancestor session and the current session.
  2. Wrote an owned plain ancestor reset archive and an owned topic-suffixed ancestor archive with matching `type: "session"` headers.
  3. Wrote newer same-prefix colliding and headerless topic reset archives in the same sessions directory.
  4. Invoked the real Gateway `chat.history` RPC path with and without `includeFamily: true`.
  5. Created a second Gateway/RPC case with 40 ancestor session ids before the current id in `usageFamilySessionIds`.
  6. Invoked embedded-mode `chat.history(includeFamily)` through the sessions tool stub, including the same >32-family-target cap case.
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal capture from current head:
  ```text
  $ CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts src/agents/tools/embedded-gateway-stub.test.ts
  Test Files  2 passed (2)
  Tests  84 passed (84)
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
  Tests  11 passed (11)
  [test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 44.69s

  $ CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
  Tests  36 passed (36)
  [test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 30.16s

  $ CI=true node --import tsx scripts/generate-prompt-snapshots.ts --check
  Prompt snapshots are current (7 files).

  $ CI=true node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
  # passed

  $ CI=true node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo
  # passed

  $ git diff --check
  # no output
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** Default `chat.history` excludes ancestor reset archive content, while `chat.history(includeFamily)` includes the owned plain ancestor archive, includes the owned topic-suffixed ancestor archive, includes current-session content, and rejects the colliding/headerless topic reset archives. In the >32-target Gateway/RPC regression, `chat.history(includeFamily)` contains `current survives family target cap`, contains early ancestor content, and does not read the ancestor entries beyond the target cap. Embedded `chat.history(includeFamily)` now reads the current active transcript first, then current archives, then ancestor archives/active transcripts; its >32-target regression also keeps the current transcript and drops over-cap ancestors.
- **What was not tested:** I did not run a live Telegram topic end-to-end, and this PR does not add prompt-level carryover summaries or automatic prompt injection. It only adds explicit family history retrieval for gateway/tool callers.

## Current head addendum: `8c164eeaa3`

ClawSweeper's `a12e1bbcf0` review finding is addressed in this head:

- Family target selection still reserves the current active transcript before the 32-target cap is reached.
- Family output order is now ancestor transcripts first, then current-session reset archives, then the current active transcript, so final `limit` trimming keeps the active session tail.
- Embedded `chat.history(includeFamily)` mirrors the Gateway order.
- Added over-limit regressions proving `includeFamily` with a small final limit keeps current active history.

Standalone redacted embedded `chat.history` smoke from current head `8c164eeaa3545a9f05dc507908f9dec0236d2c47`:

```text
$ node --import tsx --input-type=module <<'TS'
# Creates a temporary session.store, one ancestor transcript, and one current transcript;
# calls createEmbeddedCallGateway().chat.history with default, includeFamily, and includeFamily+limit.
TS
{
  "default": [
    "redacted current context"
  ],
  "includeFamily": [
    "redacted ancestor context",
    "redacted current context"
  ],
  "includeFamilyLimit1": [
    "redacted current context"
  ]
}
```

Validation on `8c164eeaa3545a9f05dc507908f9dec0236d2c47`:

```text
$ CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts src/agents/tools/embedded-gateway-stub.test.ts
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  84 passed (84)
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  11 passed (11)

$ CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts -t 'chat.history preserves current transcript when family targets hit the cap'
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  1 passed | 41 skipped (42)

$ CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tools/embedded-gateway-stub.test.ts -t 'keeps embedded current chat history when family targets hit the cap'
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  2 passed | 20 skipped (22)

$ CI=true node --import tsx scripts/generate-prompt-snapshots.ts --check
Prompt snapshots are current (7 files).

$ CI=true node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
# passed

$ CI=true node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo
# passed

$ git diff --check
# no output
```

## Current head addendum: `0d37b42702`

Maintainer readiness note addressed on current head `0d37b42702688ac9c4bf0edf949f99b00f096cf6`:

- Removed the `resetAncestors` sessions_history alias so the model-facing selector is only `includeFamily`.
- Kept Gateway `chat.history` and `sessions_history` aligned on the single `includeFamily` flag.
- The embedded history macOS path-normalization failure was already addressed by dropping the redundant path assertion in `6050d0bbfb`.
- Exact-head CI has restarted for `0d37b42702`; at refresh time there were no failed checks and the remaining required/security checks were still running.

Validation on `0d37b42702688ac9c4bf0edf949f99b00f096cf6`:

```text
$ CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  36 passed (36)

$ CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tools/embedded-gateway-stub.test.ts -t 'reads embedded chat history from reset ancestor transcripts when includeFamily is set|keeps embedded current chat history when family targets hit the cap'
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  4 passed | 18 skipped (22)

$ CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat-b.test.ts -t 'chat.history includes owned topic reset archives when includeFamily is set|chat.history preserves current transcript when family targets hit the cap'
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  1 passed | 41 skipped (42)

$ git diff --check
# no output
```

## Current head addendum: `1aa4e9cbb9`

ClawSweeper's generated-snapshot blocker is addressed on current head `1aa4e9cbb94f6841803bd55559aa0ea6de17dc67`:

- Refreshed the Codex dynamic-tool prompt snapshots after removing `resetAncestors`.
- `resetAncestors` no longer appears in source, docs, protocol/tool snapshots, or prompt snapshot counters.
- Kept `includeFamily` as the only model-facing family-history selector.

Validation on `1aa4e9cbb94f6841803bd55559aa0ea6de17dc67`:

```text
$ CI=true pnpm prompt:snapshots:gen
Wrote 7 prompt snapshot files.

$ CI=true node --import tsx scripts/generate-prompt-snapshots.ts --check
Prompt snapshots are current (7 files).

$ CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  36 passed (36)

$ git diff --check
# no output
```

commands.author: anyech

target.title: fix(agents): verify completion delivery target
target.body:
## Summary

This PR tightens ACP/subagent completion announcement delivery accounting so a completion is only counted as visibly delivered when the delivery evidence matches the requester route.

It:
- propagates outbound payload target metadata into delivery status
- validates automatic completion delivery against provider/account/channel/thread targets
- requires exact account/thread evidence for threaded origins
- preserves target metadata on failed payload outcomes for diagnostics
- keeps the current-chat generated-media compatibility path while avoiding explicit wrong-target matches

## Behavior addressed

Completion announcements can be injected through an internal/inter-session path while the original requester is a channel/thread. Broad checks such as “visible payload exists” or “message tool sent something” can count delivery as successful even when the visible delivery evidence belongs to another route.

This change makes completion delivery proof target-aware: wrong thread, missing thread, missing account, and provider mismatches no longer satisfy delivery for a concrete requester route.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: Completion delivery is only counted as visibly delivered when runtime delivery evidence matches the requester route, including concrete account/thread target. Wrong-thread message-tool evidence must be rejected; exact-thread evidence must be accepted.
- **Real setup tested**: Disposable checkout of PR branch `anyech/openclaw:fix/acp-completion-thread-delivery` under `tmp/github-open-work/real-proof-20260603T073207Z/pr-89287`. A one-off non-production runtime harness (`proof-89287.ts`, saved in the review artifact directory and disposable checkout) imported the real `src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.ts` module and called `deliverSubagentAnnouncement` through the real announce dispatch/direct-delivery path. Synthetic session store/config/origin data used a fake Discord group/thread route; only the boundary gateway/transport deps were substituted to record and return delivery envelopes. No production Gateway/config/runtime/state or private deployment proof was used.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Terminal commands run in the disposable checkout were `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile --ignore-scripts` and `node --import tsx proof-89287.ts`; full command transcript is in `review/real-proof-build-20260603T073207Z/messaging/exact-commands-used.txt`.
- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal output / runtime log excerpt from `review/real-proof-build-20260603T073207Z/messaging/evidence/proof-89287.log`:

  ```json
  {
    "agentParams": {"deliver":false,"channel":"discord","to":"channel:proof-parent","threadId":"thread-proof-expected","sourceReplyDeliveryMode":"message_tool_only"},
    "wrong": {"delivered":false,"path":"direct","reason":"message_tool_delivery_missing","error":"completion agent did not use the message tool for message-tool-only delivery"},
    "correct": {"delivered":true,"path":"direct","phases":[{"phase":"direct-primary","delivered":true,"path":"direct"}]}
  }
  ```

- **Observed result after fix**: With the same synthetic requester route, message-tool evidence for `thread-proof-wrong` was rejected/flagged as missing target delivery, while message-tool evidence for `thread-proof-expected` was accepted as direct completion delivery. This exercised the repository runtime announce/direct-delivery implementation, not only an isolated matcher function.
- **What was not tested**: No live production Gateway, live Discord workspace, real bot token, or private deployment state was used. The outbound provider and gateway agent call were substituted only at the non-production boundary so the proof remains sanitized.

target.title: fix(config): allow wildcard * as a valid channel id in channel config validation
target.body:
## Summary
- Config validation was rejecting `channels["*"]` with "unknown channel id: *"
- The `*` wildcard is a valid special case meaning "apply to all channels"
- This blocked users from configuring wildcard channel settings like `channels["*"].ackReaction`
- Fix: add `"*"` to the `allowedChannels` set in `src/config/validation.ts`
- This is a follow-up to #91857 which fixes the runtime behavior — this PR unblocks live proof for that fix

## Linked context
Closes # N/A
Related # 91857
Was this requested by a maintainer or owner? No — discovered as a blocker while raising #91857

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)
- Behavior or issue addressed: `channels["*"].ackReaction` config rejected at startup with "unknown channel id: *"
- Real environment tested: macOS, Ollama/llama3.2, ClickClack channel
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: added `channels["*"].ackReaction = "🎯"` to openclaw.json — OpenClaw now starts successfully without validation error
- Evidence after fix: 57/57 unit tests passing including 2 new tests specifically for wildcard validation
- Observed result after fix: config validation accepts `"*"` as a valid channel id
- What was not tested: all possible wildcard config combinations
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: none for this fix — validation change is directly testable

## Tests and validation
Commands run: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/config/config.plugin-validation.test.ts --reporter=verbose`

Results: 57/57 tests passing

Regression coverage added:
- "accepts wildcard * as a valid channel id in channels config"
- "still rejects unknown non-wildcard channel ids"

What failed before this fix: OpenClaw refused to start with `channels["*"]` in config

## Risk checklist
Did user-visible behavior change? Yes — config validation now accepts `"*"` as a channel id
Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? Yes — previously invalid config is now valid
Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? No
What is the highest-risk area? Accepting a new config shape that was previously rejected
How is that risk mitigated? `"*"` is explicitly allowlisted as a special case — random unknown channel ids still fail validation. New test confirms invalid ids are still rejected.

## Current review state
Ready for maintainer review.
This PR and #91857 are related — together they fully fix wildcard channel config support.

commands.author: Jerry-Xin

target.title: fix(discord): disable ws 8.21.0 receiver part limits for gateway sockets
target.body:
## Summary

ws 8.21.0 introduced `maxBufferedChunks` and `maxFragments` receiver limits (commit `[security] Limit retained message parts`) that can trigger `WS_ERR_TOO_MANY_BUFFERED_PARTS` and close connections with code 1008 under certain traffic patterns.

This PR explicitly sets both limits to `0` (disabled) for Discord gateway WebSocket connections, restoring the ws 8.20.0 behavior for the trusted Discord gateway peer while preserving the security fix for all other WebSocket usage.

## Changes

- **`extensions/discord/src/internal/gateway.ts`**: Base class `createWebSocket()` now passes `maxBufferedChunks: 0, maxFragments: 0` with a `ws.ClientOptions` cast (since `@types/ws` 8.18.1 does not yet expose these fields).
- **`extensions/discord/src/monitor/gateway-plugin.ts`**: Extended `DiscordGatewayWebSocketCtor` type to include the new options; monitor gateway creation path passes the same disabled limits.
- **`extensions/discord/src/internal/gateway.test.ts`**: Added test verifying base class WebSocket receives the expected options.
- **`extensions/discord/src/monitor/gateway-plugin.test.ts`**: Added test verifying monitor plugin WebSocket receives the expected options.
- **`extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.proxy.test.ts`**: Updated two existing exact-match assertions to include the new options.

## Why `0` instead of raising the limits

- ws 8.21.0 treats `0` as disabled (`receiver.js:61`, `receiver.js:98`: `if (this._maxBufferedChunks > 0 && ...)`), matching the pre-8.21.0 behavior exactly.
- Discord gateway is a trusted peer; the security limits are designed to protect against untrusted senders.
- Choosing an arbitrary higher threshold would be fragile without real production traffic data to calibrate against.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: ws 8.21.0's retained-part receiver limits (`maxFragments` default 131072 / `maxBufferedChunks` default 1048576) abort a Discord gateway socket with `WS_ERR_TOO_MANY_BUFFERED_PARTS` / close `1008`; this PR sets both to `0` (disabled) on the Discord gateway sockets, restoring ws 8.20.0 behavior for that trusted peer.
- **Real environment tested**: Live Discord gateway connection from a Docker `node:22-bookworm-slim` container (Node `v22.22.3`) with the repo-pinned `ws@8.21.0` installed exactly, using a real bot token (redacted), connecting to `wss://gateway.discord.gg/?v=10&encoding=json`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: build a `node:22-bookworm-slim` image with `npm install ws@8.21.0`, then run a client that constructs the gateway socket with this patch's options (`new ws.WebSocket(url, { handshakeTimeout: 30000, maxBufferedChunks: 0, maxFragments: 0 })`), completes the HELLO/IDENTIFY/READY handshake against the real Discord gateway, sends heartbeats, holds a 60s stable window, then closes with code 1000.
- **Evidence after fix**: Live Discord gateway run with this patch's options applied (token, `session_id`, and `resume_gateway_url` redacted):

```
[env] node=v22.22.3 ws=8.21.0
[discord] connecting wss://gateway.discord.gg (maxBufferedChunks:0, maxFragments:0)
[discord] open
[discord] HELLO heartbeat_interval=41250ms
[discord] IDENTIFY sent (token redacted)
[discord] READY received (session_id/resume_gateway_url redacted)
[discord] HEARTBEAT_ACK #1
[discord] stable window 60000ms complete
[discord] close code=1000 reason=<empty>
[result] PASS live: READY + 1 heartbeat ACK; no WS_ERR_TOO_MANY_BUFFERED_PARTS during stable window
```

Supplemental deterministic mechanism repro against a local ws 8.21.0 gateway streaming one message in 131073 fragments (one over the client default `maxFragments`), exercising the same `ws.WebSocket` receiver path:

```
# default ws.WebSocket (ws 8.21 defaults)
[before] open
[before] error code=WS_ERR_TOO_MANY_BUFFERED_PARTS message=Too many message fragments
[before] close code=1006 reason=<empty>

# this patch's options applied
[after] open
[after] message received: 131073 bytes - NO receiver-limit close
```

- **Observed result after fix**: On the real Discord gateway, with the disabled limits applied, the connection reaches `READY`, exchanges heartbeats, and stays connected through the 60s window with no `WS_ERR_TOO_MANY_BUFFERED_PARTS` and no `1008` close; the only close is the intentional client-initiated `1000`. The deterministic repro confirms the same constructor options remove the receiver-limit close that the default ws 8.21 client hits.
- **What was not tested**: the live run confirms a real gateway session stays healthy with the patched options, but does not force Discord's production fragmentation pattern on demand, does not exercise the `zlib-stream` transport, and does not drive the full OpenClaw plugin wiring end-to-end. The deterministic repro proves the ws 8.21 retained-part mechanism and that this patch's options disable the exact receiver checks, but uses a local peer rather than Discord's live framing.

## Tests

- `gateway.test.ts`: 25 passed
- `gateway-plugin.test.ts`: 17 passed  
- `provider.proxy.test.ts`: 21 passed
- TypeScript check (tsgo): clean

Fixes #88330

target.title: fix(googlechat): preserve thread targets through reply delivery
target.body:
## Summary
- Repair Google Chat reply delivery so text chunks, media sends, typing-message fallback, and durable final delivery use the intended current thread target without leaking stale reply metadata.
- Keep `replyToMode` semantics intact for implicit vs explicit reply targets.
- Carry forward the useful prior work from #74235 and the earlier #43112/#67055 thread-routing attempts.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/googlechat/src/monitor.reply-delivery.test.ts extensions/googlechat/src/monitor-durable.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:changed`

Refs: #42510, #69422, #70041, #74235.

ClawSweeper 🐠 replacement reef notes:
- Cluster: gitcrawl-4952-google-chat-dm-threading-broken-each-response-creates-new-thread
- Source PRs: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/74235, https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/67055, https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/43112, https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/30965
- Credit: Preserve source PR attribution for https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/74235 after live hydration identifies its author and branch writability.; Credit @banddude for the prior replyToMode/off-mode attempt in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/67055 when release-note context mentions provenance.; Credit @neeravmakwana for the earlier replyToMode/thread-routing attempt in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/43112 when release-note context mentions provenance.; Credit @novan for the landed Google Chat messageReplyOption groundwork in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/30965 if the final PR body discusses the regression history.

target.title: fix(web-ui): skip hidden subagent picker pages
target.body:
Fixes #89249.

Summary:
- Advance the chat session picker through raw `sessions.list` pages that add no visible rows after hidden spawn-child subagent filtering.
- Stop showing raw total counts when the picker is filtering out loaded rows, avoiding misleading states like `1 / 177`.
- Add browser E2E regression coverage for a hidden-only page followed by a visible session page.

Surface area:
- No public config schema/options changes.
- No plugin API/contract changes.
- No new CLI flags, env vars, migrations, or setup surface.

Validation:
- `OPENCLAW_CAPTURE_UI_PROOF=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/e2e/chat-picker-pagination.e2e.test.ts --reporter=dot` -> 1 file, 2 passed; screenshot proof captured at `.artifacts/pr-89323-control-ui-proof/chat-picker-hidden-page-skip.png` (1280x900 PNG).
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts -t "chat session picker" --reporter=dot` -> 1 file, 6 passed, 89 skipped.
- `node_modules/oxfmt/bin/oxfmt --check ui/src/ui/e2e/chat-picker-pagination.e2e.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/session-controls.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts` -> passed.
- `node_modules/oxlint/bin/oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.json ui/src/ui/e2e/chat-picker-pagination.e2e.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/session-controls.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts` -> 0 warnings/errors.
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD && git diff --check` -> passed.

Real behavior proof:
- Added a Playwright/Chromium Control UI E2E test that drives the actual chat picker in a browser against the mocked Gateway Control UI server.
- The test creates a visible first page plus a hidden subagent row, then clicks Load More. The mocked backend returns a hidden-only raw page at offset `2`; the picker automatically requests offset `4` and renders the next visible top-level chat.
- Assertions cover the requested offsets (`2`, then `4`), the visible picker count (`2`), absence of hidden subagent options, and removal of the Load More button after all visible rows are loaded.
- Proof environment: WSL Ubuntu 24.04, Playwright Chromium, local `.artifacts/pr-89323-control-ui-proof/chat-picker-hidden-page-skip.png` screenshot from the passing browser run.

Signed head:
- `c6b0342f48c4a67d475d40e35edd40110a95cbcc` (`gpgsig` present)

target.title: test(plugins): cover filtered bundle MCP servers
target.body:
## Summary

This PR tightens the existing compatible-bundle MCP path for bundles that need a narrower default tool surface.

- Adds regression coverage that a Codex-compatible bundle `.mcp.json` preserves `toolFilter.include`, `toolFilter.exclude`, and a relative bundle data root.
- Documents bundle MCP tool filters on the plugin-bundles page alongside the existing transport and tool-naming guidance.
- Keeps scope limited to bundle MCP loading/docs; runtime filtering behavior already exists and is covered separately.

Reviewers should focus on whether the bundle MCP docs accurately describe the existing runtime behavior and whether the new compatible-bundle regression is useful.

## Linked context

No issue filed.

Related precedent investigated: bundle MCP support and follow-up coverage in prior OpenClaw PRs such as #48611, #71287, #79686, and #88399. This PR follows that existing bundle MCP seam.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: Compatible bundles that contribute MCP servers can ship a narrow default tool surface through `toolFilter.include` and `toolFilter.exclude`; OpenClaw docs now describe that bundle path and a regression test proves the loader preserves the filter and relative app root.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, branch `test/filtered-bundle-mcp`, commit `d5bffb3dc0`, with an isolated temporary `HOME`, `OPENCLAW_HOME`, and `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Created a temporary Codex-compatible bundle containing `.codex-plugin/plugin.json`, `.mcp.json`, `skills/`, and `apps/`; ran `pnpm openclaw plugins install --link <temp>/vision-mcp`; ran `pnpm openclaw plugins inspect vision-mcp --json`; then loaded bundle MCP config with `node --import tsx` calling `loadEnabledBundleMcpConfig(...)` against the isolated home/config.

Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the temporary real OpenClaw setup:

```text
Proof root: /var/folders/.../openclaw-bundle-proof.gE7Fjt
$ node scripts/run-node.mjs plugins install --link /var/folders/.../openclaw-bundle-proof.gE7Fjt/vision-mcp
Linked plugin path: /var/folders/.../openclaw-bundle-proof.gE7Fjt/vision-mcp
Restart the gateway to load plugins.
$ node scripts/run-node.mjs plugins inspect vision-mcp --json
{
  "diagnostics": [],
  "command": "npx",
  "args": [
    "-y",
    "@vision-mcp/cli@latest",
    "serve",
    "--apps-root",
    "/private/var/folders/.../openclaw-bundle-proof.gE7Fjt/vision-mcp/apps"
  ],
  "cwdEndsWith": true,
  "toolFilter": {
    "include": [
      "capsule.*",
      "vision_map.snapshot",
      "vision_map.list_apps",
      "vision_map.list_workflows",
      "vision_map.describe_workflow",
      "vision_map.describe",
      "vision_map.detect_state",
      "vision_map.list_actions",
      "vision_map.describe_action",
      "vision_map.perform_action",
      "vision_map.run_workflow",
      "vision_map.verify",
      "vision_map.repair_minimal",
      "vision_map.export_trace"
    ],
    "exclude": [
      "vision_map.click_at",
      "vision_map.type_text",
      "vision_map.press_key",
      "vision_map.scroll",
      "vision_map.init",
      "vision_map.apply_patch",
      "vision_map.add_control",
      "vision_map.commit_*",
      "vision_map.harvest_session"
    ]
  }
}
```

Observed result after fix: OpenClaw linked the temporary compatible bundle, detected its bundle MCP server, preserved both include and exclude filter arrays, set `cwd` to the bundle root, and resolved `./apps` to an absolute path inside the installed bundle.

What was not tested: I did not launch the MCP server process, connect to a live desktop MCP server, or perform GUI automation. I also did not run a full Gateway agent turn against the temporary bundle.

Proof limitations or environment constraints: The proof uses a temporary local compatible bundle and the real OpenClaw plugin installer/loader, but the MCP server command itself is not started because this PR changes documentation and loader regression coverage, not MCP runtime execution behavior.

## Tests and validation

Commands run:

```text
node scripts/test-projects.mjs -- src/plugins/bundle-mcp.test.ts --reporter=verbose
pnpm format:check src/plugins/bundle-mcp.test.ts
git diff --check
pnpm check:docs
codex review --base origin/main
```

Regression coverage added: `src/plugins/bundle-mcp.test.ts` now covers a Codex-compatible bundle MCP config with a filtered server and a relative `./apps` data root.

Validation results:

```text
src/plugins/bundle-mcp.test.ts: 1 file passed, 8 tests passed
pnpm format:check src/plugins/bundle-mcp.test.ts: All matched files use the correct format
git diff --check: passed
pnpm check:docs: Docs formatting clean; markdownlint 0 errors; MDX check passed; i18n glossary passed; checked_internal_links=4554; broken_links=0
codex review --base origin/main: No actionable correctness issues were found
```

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`): No runtime behavior change; docs now describe existing bundle MCP filter behavior.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`): No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`): No runtime change. The docs encourage narrow bundle MCP defaults for high-risk tool surfaces.

What is the highest-risk area? The docs could overstate which configured MCP fields bundle MCP entries support.

How is that risk mitigated? Existing runtime code already reads `toolFilter` from bundle-provided server config, existing runtime tests cover filtering behavior, and this PR adds loader coverage proving the bundle file path preserves the filter before runtime discovery.

## Current review state

AI-assisted: yes.

Next action: maintainer review.
